Name: Leslie Sacks Gallery
Description:
Leslie J. Sacks established his first gallery, Les Art International in Johannesburg, South Africa, in 1981.  Upon emigrating to the United States, he opened Leslie Sacks Fine Art in Los Angeles in 1992, which became an important American venue specializing in fine art editions and rare works on paper by Modern, Post-War and Contemporary American and European masters.  In 2007 Leslie Sacks Fine Art acquired Bobbie Greenfield Gallery in the Bergamot Station Arts Center in Santa Monica, California thereby establishing a second venue in Leslie Sacks Gallery.  This space focused on editions and works on paper by Post-War and Contemporary masters and represented the Estate of Robert Motherwell (the Dedalus Foundation).  The gallery further represented significant mid-career and established Los Angeles Contemporary artists.

Leslie Sacks passed away in 2013.  With over three-decades in the art world, his legacy now lives on through the singular venue at Leslie Sacks Gallery.  With a venerable history and reputation the gallery continues its well-established exhibition program and unrivaled inventory of Post-War and Contemporary works on paper and editions.  This is complimented by a special collection of Modern master works, as was Leslie Sacks’ deep passion as both a collector and a dealer.

Leslie Sacks Gallery is a member of the International Fine Print Dealers Association (IFPDA), Art Dealers Association of California (ADAC) and Fine Art Dealers Association (FADA).  The gallery participates in major art fairs including Art Miami, and the IFPDA Print Fair in New York and Miami.

Description:
یک نشریهی مجازی دربارهی هنرهای تجسمی. اینجا از گالریگردیها و نمایشگاههای روز، هنرمندان و بازتاب فعالیتهایشان در جهان یادداشتهایی ثبت میشود. گاهی هم از مسائل روز و حواشی آنها، آن قدر که میدانیم مینویسیم.
همهی این یادداشتها حاصل نظرات شخصی نویسندگان اُکرمگ است، بدون اعمال سیاست یا جهتگیری خاص.

Name: Glassworks Studio
Description:
Glassworks Studio, in Downtown Morristown, NJ, was the first walk-in all glass fusing studio open to the public, novice and artist alike. Since then, Glassworks has grown, transitioned, improved and refined the experience.

No experience is necessary and all ages are welcome. The trained and friendly associates will teach you all that you need to know to get started. Glassworks is a great space for people to gather and to spend quality time together - and you will create a wonderful glass product. Glassworks hosts groups, team events, birthday parties, and more, as well as offering open studio time, and creating custom commission pieces.

Name: Chester Art Centre NS
Description:
The Chester Art Centre was founded in April 2007 to enhance and encourage artistic opportunities in the Municipality of Chester, Nova Scotia.
 
The Chester Art Centre offers a wide range of exhibits year-round in a classic Chester cottage which features a main floor exhibition space, accessible bathroom and is fully licensed ; the upstairs is the Art Centre office. The Chester Art Centre Annex at 5 Pleasant street offers a varied and experiential range of classes and workshops for children and adults. We also offer art experiences throughout the community.

The Chester Art Centre sponsors the hugely successful Chester Creates, an exciting free two-day festival highlighted by a featured month-long exhibition, stimulating demonstrations and art experiences, live music, and a chance to meet local and regional artists and buy their works.

We finance the Art Centre and our educational efforts through art classes, the art fair and festival, and the generous contributions of supporters, sponsors and various government programs.

We partner with the Recreation Department of the Municipality of Chester and have co-ventured on programs with the Chester Playhouse, Chester Elementary School, Chester Area Middle School, the Chester Municipal Heritage Society, the Ross Farm Museum, Our Health Centre and more.

Name: Radius Art Studio
Description:
Radius Art Studio is a creative community offering ceramics workshops, clay studio memberships, and a handmade retail shop. Our vision is to offer work space and ceramics education for all skill levels in a fun and supportive environment.

We believe that creativity is power, and our vision is to provide opportunities for everyone to explore that power within themselves. We are artists, makers, and friends who welcome the contributions and perspectives of all people.

We also partner with other artists and art organizations to cross promote art events and workshops beyond the medium of clay.

Radius began in an apartment in 2002, with a simple mission of offering art classes to the local community. This mission continues today under the vision and stewardship of Korin Noelle. Korin joined the studio as an artist in 2008, and became owner/operator of this iconic Portland establishment in 2014.

In October of 2021, Radius Art Studio moved from our Morrison studio and our Burnside Annex shop and opened up the current studio space located at 2324 SE Belmont St.

Name: WARART
Description: WARART is an online art-gallery aimed to share all forms of art devoted to the war in Ukraine.

Name: CAMILLE ROUSSEL
Description:
Artiste textile et designer, Camille Roussel est diplômée en design objet de l’École Nationale Supérieure des Arts Décoratifs de Paris. En 2011 elle crée son studio de créations et développe des collections de bijoux, d’accessoires et d’objets en cuir.

Aujourd'hui spécialisée dans l'éco-conception et les textiles bio-sourcés elle façonne ses pièces dans toutes les dimensions, à grande ou petites échelles. Les bijoux sont des micro-architectures et ses sculptures en textiles, des espaces sensorielles ludiques, invitant à la déambulation.

Camille aborde l’objet, le design et l’espace de façon picturale attachant une grande importance à la couleur et à la composition. Tel des jeux de constructions, ses installations plastiques tout comme ses objets, sont composés de modules qui s’assemblent et s’entrelacent pour constituer d'autres dimensions de formes.

Name: Institute for Contemporary Art, Zagreb
Description:
First established as SCCA - Zagreb in 1993,  Institute for Contemporary Art | Institut za suvremenu umjetnost was founded in 1998 by 13 co-founding members.

We are now Zagreb based independent cultural organisation running a gallery, reading room and archive. We work to support the development of visual arts and artists, offering a space for exhibitions, public programs, talks, new ideas and experiments.
Our programs and activities include curating and exhibition making, research, publishing and educational programs in the field of management for artists.

In 2002 we have established the annual award for young visual artists - Radoslav Putar Award. In collaboration with Zagreb based organisations, we organise Gallery Weekend Zagreb since 2015.

Located in the very centre of the city, we may provide you with all you need to know about the contemporary visual arts in Croatia.

[Our new website www.institute.hr is currently in construction, so may follow us here and on the FB as ICA.Zagreb]

Name: Broadway in Tucson
Description:
Broadway in Tucson/A Nederlander Presentation is part of the nationally recognized Nederlander Producing Company of America. Broadway in Tucson’s debut season opened in September 2004 with the Arizona premiere of Movin’ Out at the Tucson Music Hall. Since that first season, Broadway in Tucson has brought more than 1,100,000 patrons to the touring Broadway experience.

Broadway in Tucson holds performances at Centennial Hall, 1020 E University Blvd, Tucson, Arizona, United States.

Name: Christine Klassen Gallery | CKG
Description:
Christine Klassen Gallery | CKG, represents a dynamic group of artists united by their craft-intensive approach to art-making. CKG showcases a wide survey of artworks including painting, sculpture, ceramics, drawing, photography and other media formats. From emerging to established artists, CKG represents artists in all points of their creative careers, and strives to stimulate gallery visitors through consistent innovative projects and exhibitions.

In February 2014 CKG relocated to a 4600 sq. foot mid-century warehouse space in the Manchester Industrial area just off Macleod Trail to become one of Calgary’s largest commercial gallery spaces. The gallery has been a cornerstone of Calgary’s art community since 2007.

Name: Artilubi
Description: Artilubi a pour objectif de mettre en lien les professionnels du secteur culturel, les artistes et le public et de répondre à leurs besoins en proposant des produits et services innovants !

Name: ARTS AU PLURIAILES
Description: ARTS AU PLURIAILES is a performing arts company based out of Baie-Mahault in Guadeloupe, Guadeloupe, France.

Name: K's Art Genesis
Description:
K’s Art Genesis is where creativity knows no bounds — for kids and adults alike! 

Whether you're picking up a paintbrush for the first time, diving into a corporate team-building art workshop, or looking for a unique commissioned piece, we’re here to help you unleash your inner artist. Our art classes and workshops are designed to bring out the childlike joy in every participant, sparking imagination, exploration, and fun in every stroke.

We offer:

- Art Workshops for Adults – Tap into your creativity and discover a new hobby.
- Art Classes for Kids – Fun, interactive classes that inspire young minds.
- Corporate Art Workshops – Team-building experiences that encourage collaboration and creative problem-solving.
- Custom Commissioned Art – Unique artwork created just for you.

At K’s Art Genesis, we believe that everyone has an artist inside waiting to be discovered. So come on in, get messy, and let your creativity run wild!

Join us to create, learn, and rediscover the joy of art, no matter your age!

Name: R Young Art
Description: Fine art independent family run gallery with a difference in the centre of Wokingham. We have a large range of artwork on display: - Originals - Ltd Editions - Prints - Sculptures and ceramics. We also offer a range of art related services: - Scanning - Giclée printing - Events - Consulting - Restorations

Name: COVert Arts
Description: COVert Arts is an artists’ collective that is committed to shine light on Coventry and Warwickshire hidden works of art. We aim to partner with like-minded businesses in easily accessible locations that are excited to showcase the exceptional art of Coventry that often remains out of view. Through this effort we will create opportunities and foster a sense of inclusivity and togetherness for all communities of Coventry by cherishing everyone’s artistic input.

Name: PYROS KARE
Description: Fine arts club of Kalasalingam University provides an opportunity to the students to let their imagination run wild and provides them with the sight to see things in a different way. Students learn from one another and share their prowess in different aspects of art.

Name: The Painted Cloud
Description:
Philosophy: The Painted Cloud is an art studio that practices community-minded experimentation in the arts. Through experimental processes, we develop foundations that change our perspectives, help us become more open-minded, and help us gain autonomy. Experimenting in a group setting broadens our perspectives of what is possible within ourselves, our peers, and our communities. Gaining confidence in trying new things allows us to have more open minds toward our own intuition and creativity, that of our peers, and that of our communities. Understanding our power and ability to contribute to our own well-being and the well-being of our communities gives us autonomy without losing sight of our responsibility to our studio mates and those we collaborate with regularly. Community-minded experimentation is responsible, ethical artistic development and adventure. 

The Painted Cloud offers an invigorating environment for young and maturing artists to learn, grow, and develop their desire to experiment with materials while expanding their skill-sets and broadening their perspectives. 

Process and dialogue are at the heart of our activities and experimentations.

We place an emphasis on process to support all forms of creativity, offering a space for artists of every age to foster free-thinking, to engage in conversation, and to build self-confidence. Teaching artists map projects for young and maturing artists to work both independently and collaboratively over the course of the session series. Engaging in independent work allows young and maturing artists to develop foundational tools and harness their intuitive decision-making abilities. Participating in collaborative work prompts young and maturing artists in finding their individual roles within their The Painted Cloud community, linking to their pursuits of finding their roles in their greater communities beyond their time working with The Painted Cloud.

Name: BüroSarıgedik
Description:
BüroSarıgedik is a new venture in developing support structures for artists.

BüroSarıgedik originates from the desire and the need to respond to current conditions of the local and international art scene, to work with artists from İstanbul in a novel format of (intimate, personal, etc.) artist representation. BüroSarıgedik will organize local and international projects, sourcing funding for production & fabrication of new works and publications by artists, liaising with institutions, and placing work in public and trustworthy collections.

BüroSarıgedik represents: Murat Akagündüz, CANAN, Ergin Çavuşoğlu, Cengiz Çekil, Gülsün Karamustafa, SENA and Erdil Yaşaroğlu.
________________________________________________________________________
BüroSarıgedik sanatçılara destek yapıları oluşturmak amacıyla yola çıkan yeni bir girişim.

BüroSarıgedik de bir proje olarak sanat ortamının güncel koşullarına yönelik yanıt geliştirebilme, İstanbullu sanatçılarla daha yakın ve sağlam bir sanatçı temsili modeli çerçevesinde çalışma arzusu ve gereksinimiyle yola çıkıyor. BüroSarıgedik yerel ve uluslararası ölçekte sergiler düzenleme, yeni yapıtların üretimi için fon yaratma, sanatçılarla ilgili yayınlar hazırlama, kurumlarla işbirlikleri geliştirme ve yapıtları kamusal ve güvenilir nitelikteki özel koleksiyonlara katma gibi faaliyetler yürütmeyi hedefliyor.

BüroSarıgedik şu anda Murat Akagündüz, CANAN, Ergin Çavuşoğlu, Cengiz Çekil, Gülsün Karamustafa, SENA ve Erdil Yaşaroğlu’nu temsil etmektedir.

Name: Lucky 7s Tattoo
Description: Full custom tattoo studio. Great , clean, fun shop. We are 4 PROFESSIOAL TATTOOERS. Best customer service around. Come by and Check us out.

Name: Ruby Fear Artist
Description:
There is a deep inner need to create, to bring into existence that which is yet to be discovered. Art is, and always has been, my way to communicate and express what language cannot; it is my way of sharing, connecting, healing, and building relationships beyond the paint brush. 

When I paint, it transforms my inner self and invites the observers to participate in this journey with me. There is a fierce passion, deep inside my soul, that drives me to engage with oil, acrylics, and mixed media. Every movement, texture, interaction between light and dark, color and shape, form and line, is an exhilarating experience. At times painful, but every time extraordinary.

Name: David Baldwin Art
Description:
David Baldwin // visual artist and experimental musician.

David’s acrylic on canvas painting is about personal honesty, letting go of the trickery of technique, and allowing ideas and feelings to come to the surface in the most distilled, pure manner. Simple shapes and colors create feelings and movement that evoke the complex hidden inside the simple. He hopes that these paintings are fun and easy to live with!

Baldwin has painted his entire life and is excited to take this passion to the next level. Also, he is an avid cyclist and musician. If not painting or riding his bike around Sonoma County, he is creating music under the name “Unhowto,” which is available through all streaming platforms.

Born and raised in Southern California, David now resides in Santa Rosa, California.

Name: InSitu Works
Description:
InSitu Works is the online marketplace and advisory for fine art and design. We provide a platform where collectors can learn about and acquire artworks from emerging and established artists around the world.

OUR STORY:
A collaboration between two close friends, InSitu Works was conceived as an alternative to help aspiring collectors find high-quality artwork and grant emerging artists the exposure they deserve. InSitu Works is sponsored by the Rockefeller Brothers Fund and Artspire (part of the New York Foundation for the Arts). When you acquire art through InSitu Works, you contribute directly to the promotion and development of emerging artists.

OUR SERVICES:
Advisory for designers, developers, and institutional buyers 
InSitu Works streamlines the often times fragmented process of sourcing art. We approach each new project with the goal of showing work in compelling spaces that provoke emotion and engage guests both visually and intellectually. By understanding our clients’ needs and leveraging our design, hospitality and art know-how, we allow for a hassle-free experience in transforming any space into an intriguing display of art.  

Advisory for individual collectors
Whether you are an experienced or aspiring collector, InSitu Works is here to help you reach your art collecting goals. We provide personalized art advisory and procurement services to help you start, build or maintain your art collection.

Our services include but are not limited to:

-         Acquisition advisory and guidance

-         Access to primary & secondary markets as well as private collections

-         Negotiations with dealers

-         Representation at auction

-         Opportunities to connect with emerging artists, collectors and other figures in the art world

Name: Canvas Prints Wall Art - Candlesbook Store
Description:
Best sellers of Vintage Book Cover Art Print on Canvas and Posters. 

Candlesbook.com was founded in 2008 and dedicated to supply high quality posters and canvas art prints that will bring your walls to speak up by triggering conversations, be it in your home, appartment, office or a classroom. Attractive, affordable and durable! Check it out :-)

We strive to bring to you the best selection of vintage books and illustrations canvases, exactly as you see them in Barnes & Noble bookshops.

A ready to hang canvas print is an affordable alternative to an original work of art. 

Sealed with a UV coated finish protects the image from dust, moisture and fading. 

Our selection of prints includes inspirational and specialty posters, decorative prints, art reproductions and classic vintage book covers alongside with children’s literature and nursery rhymes.

Stretch your favorite art on canvas. 

Create your own gallery with the stylish look of stretched canvas. 

Each print is transferred to artist-grade cotton canvas. This unique “Museum Wrap” provides a clean, finished look from front, side and back. The canvas is wrapped around 1½” wooden support bars to create a finished look that allows you to hang the piece without a frame.

Through our excellent customer service, you can shop with confidence as you are covered by our 100% satisfaction guaranteed or money back, no questions asked, refund policy. 

We are convinced that you will be thrilled with your purchase and if, for any reasons it is not so, you may return it within 30 days and receive a free replacement or a full refund for the price paid. 

Do not hesitate to email us questions, comments and suggestions at info @ candlesbook.com 

We look forward to have you join the ranks of our satisfied and loyal customers. 

Janice Dugas -

Name: Artpce
Description: The primary mission of Artpce is and always will be to uplift and celebrate Black people and black culture. Many of her pieces highlight black joy, self-care, social justice issues as well as uplift and support black-owned businesses. Through Artpce, Halimah has had the opportunity of donating funds to organizations such as the After School Allstars to help expand the learning day for low-income children and she has created art for large companies such as Missguided UK. Artpce has been featured by many influential pages such as Revolt TV, Refinery 29, and most recently the NAACP. Be sure to visit @artpce on Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook to find more work and upcoming projects.

Name: Left of Center Art Gallery
Description:
MISSION STATEMENT

With a focus on inclusion and diversity, our goal is to make the arts accessible to all segments of our population.

What We Do

We create environments to explore artistic expressions for emerging, established, local and guest artists with our dedicated artist studios and contemporary gallery. Our Museum Collection of Authentic African Art Exhibition inspires and informs the imagination and intellect. We are dedicated to provide artists opportunities for professional growth through seminars, classes and workshops. We nurture emerging artists through mentorship and support established artists with networking and exhibitions in our gallery and throughout in the community.

We serve as advocates for the arts in public education, working with community organizations, students and art educators to enhance their curriculum offerings.

Left of Center Art Gallery is an unique and vital alternative gallery whose vision is to inspire, educate, and engage with communities through the arts. We work to share the many ways in which the arts influence and enrich our lives. With a focus on diversity, our goal is to make the arts accessible to all segments of our population by providing a welcoming gallery environment with opportunities for interactions with artists as well as opportunities to personally experience the creative process.

Artwork by Orlando Montenegro, titled Fall Under the Influence.

Name: J. Daniel Portraiture & Fine Art
Description: J. Daniel Portraiture & Fine Art represents distinguished portrait artists of national prominence and assists clients as they select various artists for commissioned portraiture. Exceptional service to clients and artists and the ability to create a trusting personal relationship with clients are the hallmarks of Jean's career as a renowned art executive and consultant.

Name: Visalia Arts Consortium, Inc.
Description: The Arts Consortium works to establish the arts as a vital part of everyday life for Tulare County residents and its visitors.  We host and promote regional events which help celebrate our diverse histories and cultures.  We thrive with the support of our artists, organizations and arts enthusiasts.

Name: Connemara Creative Arts Centre
Description:
Connemara Creative Arts Centre is a leader in the creative economy, providing educational resources and opportunities for artists and creators. Unlock your creative potential and immerse yourself in a haven of inspiration where art comes to life and imagination knows no bounds. We believe in the power of creativity and its potential to shape and transform lives. We are honoured to partner with Ealaín na bhFiann, providing an environment where people can express themselves through the creative arts.

The best creative arts workshops, art courses and weekly Creative Arts Club for kids, teenagers and adults. Connemara Artists in Residence programme supporting Irish and International visual artists. Connemara Creative Arts Summer Camp. Community outreach programme and online creative arts courses for schools, nursing homes, youth groups and rehabilitation centres.

Our mission is to inspire and nurture the creative spirit of all ages while fostering communication, collaboration and critical thinking. Everyone has the potential to be creative and to make positive contributions to our Global Community.

The artists can approach us to build a special financial program for them, from the pre-financing of their projects to internationally promoting their art on the first scene.

Galleries with a clear voice can approach us to design a financial strategy allowing them to develop and expand, while overcoming any challenges.

Art Collectors and their advisors can approach us in order to build an art portfolio fit for any type of taste or risk appetite. For inquiries email us: office@henrimaillardet.com

Name: Paul PASTAUD O.V.V
Description: Depuis 2017, la Maison de ventes Paul PASTAUD organise plus d’une centaine de ventes aux enchères publiques par an : Tableaux, bijoux, mobilier, sculptures mais aussi véhicules et matériels professionnels sont passés au feu des enchères.

Name: American Guild of Organists -- Milwaukee Chapter
Description: Professional Non-Profit Organization: The Milwaukee Chapter of the American Guild of Organists was chartered on May 5, 1919, to serve and support the professional activities of organists and church musicians in the Milwaukee area. Persons who participate in our chapter are organists, choir directors, organ builders, technicians, clergy, students, and those who appreciate organ and sacred music. The Chapter is open to those of all levels of playing proficiency.  Members especially appreciate the participation of those who simply listen and support organ and choral concerts.

Name: GalleryGift
Description: GalleryGift is an online art gallery that specialises in original, hand-made art as gifts for people. With our unique CoFund feature, “pitching in” for an artwork is made easy and becomes a meaningful, personal and affordable gift for a loved one, or even yourself- perfect for those larger occasions such as weddings, anniversaries, engagement parties, baby showers, housewarmings and milestone birthdays. Once received, the recipient of the CoFund then chooses the artwork themselves, so you know they will love it and it will no doubt become a precious memento of that special time in their life. We are excited to see GalleryGift change the stereotype of fine art by finally making it affordable, accessible, appealing and justified for everyone! Head to our website to learn more and check out our growing number of talented artists and stunning artworks

Name: DramaTech Theatre
Description: DramaTech offers a thriving theatrical laboratory environment at Georgia Tech that enriches the greater Atlanta community through innovative and diverse live performances.  Founded in 1947, we are the oldest continually running theatre in Atlanta.

Name: Hang It Services Ltd
Description:
With decades of experience in interior design, we at Hang It Services have the knowhow to get any artwork hung anywhere. We pride ourselves on our excellent customer service and offer a full curation service to help ensure that everything is hung right first time.

Started just in 2018, Hang It has grown with astonishing speed to become the areas premier artwork installation service. We serve a number of local galleries, framers and interior designers with cost-effective, accurate installations every day. 

If you'd like to talk to a professional installation expert, call today.

Name: Hagopian Arts Philadelphia Fine Art And Mural Company
Description:
Hagopian Arts is an Armenian fine art and mural collective based out of both Philadelphia and Vermont.  We are a family artist team (Kala Hagopian & father Philip Hagopian), creating unique works ranging from small paintings to large scale murals.  Our style is based in  traditional fine art while integrating modern painterly aesthetics.  Although our work varies in subject we draw inspiration from nature and often juxtapose realistic elements with colorful shapes and patterns.
Philip Hagopian’s work ranges from impressionistic figurative, landscape, and still life painting to multi-dimensional surrealism.  He exhibits nationally and internationally and his works are featured in the permanent collections of many distinguished collectors.

Kala Hagopian, co-founder of the former Fine Art Collective, Chroma Dolls LLC has worked with numerous education and community outreach programs such as The Philadelphia Mural Arts Program, The Philadelphia Women’s Conference, Cancer Treatment Centers of America, Zappos Art, Beautify Earth, The Hillside School, The Philadelphia Animal Welfare Society, RAW:Natural Born Artists, Shore2Love, Center For The Emerging Visual Artists, Project Street Art, The Youth Violence Reduction Partnership, Sobriety Through Out-Patient, JEVS Human Services, The American Foundation For Suicide Prevention, Horizon House, Fairton Correctional Institute, at-risk youth and adults, The Boys & Girls Club, as well as a range of participants of all ages. Along with community projects, Hagopian Arts works with individual clients, small businesses, and large corporations, creating custom murals that enhance and transform their environment.

Hagopian Arts combine their aesthetic and diverse backgrounds to create truly unique works of art in the form of small and large scale paintings, murals, and public art pieces.

Name: The Key Art Gallery
Description: The vision of the Key Art Gallery project is to promote the development of Arts & culture in The Gambia; inculcating arts and artistic expression as part of the Gambian identity; To promote the socio-economic status of artist in the Gambia more specifically women artist and young artist. The project is to facilitate a paradigm shift associated with art and culture towards a positive path for development and improving the lives of the women and young Gambians through frequent exhibition and advocating.

Name: AdMatrix Inc.
Description:
Ken Church is full time professional wildlife artist from York, England. Primarily interested in large canvas acrylic paintings of wildlife and nature. Essentially realistic his paintings are in a modern style of bold brush strokes, bright colors and luminous patterns of light. The subject matter of his work is a platform to explore the camouflaging effect of light and color that blend together in a tapestry of animal and fauna. 

The spirit of his work serves to draw attention to the majesty and beauty of wildlife on our planet and the need for preservation and restoration of their habitat.

Ken graduated from the York School of Art in England, and moved to the U.S. where he received a scholarship to study fine art at Brigham Young University. He moved on to Post graduate studies in drawing and illustration at The Art Center College of Design in California. 

Church began his career as a very prolific full time artist in June of 2015 after a long career as a Creative Director in several advertising agencies. His paintings began to sell immediately on-line at saatchiart.com and on fineartamerica.com. Several paintings are on display in commercial office buildings and in private 
collections. His proven capability to produce art, coupled with an extensive knowledge of advertising and marketing bodes well for future productivity and a successful career as a professional artist.

Name: Fujairah Fine Arts Academy
Description: Fujairah Fine Arts Academy is a leading cultural and educational institution dedicated to nurturing creativity and talent in music, visual arts, and ballet. We offer world-class programs, including an academic diploma in musical arts accredited by the UAE Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research, for learners of all ages and abilities, fostering artistic excellence and cultural appreciation in the UAE and beyond.

Name: Addo
Description:
Addo is a not-for-profit arts organisation that offers Curatorial, Consultancy and Project Management Services towards the delivery of innovative contemporary art in the public realm.

Founded by Sarah Pace and Tracy Simpson, we have over 20 years experience of working in the arts in Wales and beyond with a dynamic and established practice and critical knowledge of initiating, commissioning and producing a broad range of contemporary art projects. Working with and on behalf of artists, community groups, voluntary organisations, and private and public sectors partners we have curated, managed and produced: permanent and temporary public artworks and programmes; artist residencies; strategy & framework documents; research, evaluation and feasibility studies; and a variety of exhibitions and events in art and non-art spaces.

Name: The Children's Art Studio
Description:
The Children’s Art Studio is founded on the belief that children reap tremendous emotional, developmental and intellectual gains from making art and that this opportunity should be made available to all.  Small class size, thoughtful instruction and the inventive use of high quality materials is what it is all about.  

Since 2008 the Children’s Art Studio has created innovative visual arts programming, bringing the highest standards of art education to the students of Washington, DC.  During the past twelve months, we have enrolled one hundred fifty children in summer camps, two hundred children in workshops, twenty five teachers in professional development sessions, ten teens in our internship program and almost three hundred children in classes.  We hope your child will join us!

Our operations are guided by the same values that guide our programming: diligence, inclusion, integrity and care.  We are committed to the responsible stewardship of resources and aim to build an environment where every person feels safe, respected and cherished.

The Children’s Art Studio has a vision for  the youngest residents of Washington DC where all will enjoy the freedom and power that comes from making art.  In 2017 the Children’s Art Studio was invited to partner with the Promise Neighborhood Initiative to create art programs for the youngest children of Kenilworth-Parkside. We were honored by the invitation and delighted to bring the excellence of the Children’s Art Studio programs to ward 7.    By providing consistent, high quality, long term visual arts instruction, the Children’s Art Studio can mitigate the harmful effects of living in poverty and bring joy to this community.

Name: Subex Galeria d'Art
Description:
Galeria d'art fundada l'any 1973. En aquest temps Subex s’ha convertit en una de les galeries d’art figuratiu de referència a Barcelona i a tot Catalunya, mentre que artistes com José Berruezo, Jorge Duarte, Comes Busquets o Joan Pujol han consagrat la seva carrera durant el seu pas per la galeria.
 
Actualment, Subex comença una nova etapa. A partir d'aquesta tardor la galeria ofereix una sèrie de cursos de pintura impartits pels artistes que hi exposen habitualment.
 
Subex compta amb una ubicació geogràfica privilegiada. Situada al bell mig del centre de Barcelona, concretament al carrer Mallorca, entre dues de les vies més importants de la ciutat: el Passeig de Gràcia i Rambla Catalunya, i envoltada d’obres emblemàtiques de Gaudí com La Pedrera o La Casa Batlló.

Name: Grzegorz Batycki Art Gallery
Description:
Born in 1975 in Gdynia, an artist, economist, passionate about the Chinese art, language and culture. Between 2005 and 2011 studied artistic education at the Academy of Fine Arts in Gdansk. Multiple times he has been mentioning the great influence professor Mieczyslaw Olszewski, in charge of the Painting Department at that time, had on him.
Batycki has showed his art in a “Od czasu do czasu” art gallery in Gdynia, in auctions of “Mloda Sztuka” (Young Art) organized by the Sopot Fine Arts Auction House. 2018 brought with a major art showing “Fade Out Ausblenden” in Dessous gallery in Vienna (together with Soygul Boyraz). Batycki’s interests focus on drawings which are close to a compulsory act, and deeply refer to the art traditions developing in China and Japan since the 6th century AD.
“I admire traditional drawings in ink. It comes from my love for simplicity. It also includes the philosophy of Tao which divides the world into two colours. I follow a rule of “the harder, the better”. And by eliminating colours, I am trying to touch the very essence. Thus, the recipient can focus on the content, and not the form itself”, Batycki says.
The artist, for many years, has been exploring not only drawing techniques, but various work methods and the art creation  process itself. The characters in his art are random passers by, celebrities, friends and family, and above all the artist himself. He systematically explores his own emotions, tiredness, joy and pain. Hence, he creates his own private art journal.

Marek Nowak

Name: GALERIE DES MODERNES

Philippe BISMUTH, après des études universitaires en Sciences Economiques et un diplôme de second cycle de l’Ecole du Louvre, a pratiqué l’expertise en tableaux et sculptures du XXe siècle pendant dix ans auprès de plusieurs Commissaires-priseurs et notamment du Crédit Municipal de Paris, avant de se consacrer pleinement à son métier de marchand d’art moderne.

Vincent AMIAUX possède une maîtrise en Droit Privé ainsi qu’un diplôme de 3e cycle (D.E.A.) d’Histoire de l’Art. Il est spécialiste du Cubisme et a travaillé sur l’œuvre d’Albert Gleizes.

Philippe Bismuth et Vincent Amiaux se sont associés pour faire profiter leur clientèle de leur savoir et de leur expérience du marché de l’art. Dans leur galerie, ils font partager leur goût et leur sensibilité à un public varié d’amateurs, de collectionneurs éclairés et d’ institutions françaises et étrangères.

La Galerie des Modernes est membre du Syndicat National des Antiquaires (SNA) et du Carré Rive Gauche.

Name: THE ART OF ANIME
Description:
Ce fond privé de documents d'art contemporain japonais se consacre, au travers de grandes expositions culturelles, d'études, de conseil en acquisition et de conférences sous le label THE ART OF ANIME, à la reconnaissance d'un patrimoine mondial historique et culturel.

L'animation japonaise télévisuelle est devenue un vecteur global d'identification culturelle bien avant la reconnaissance des bandes dessinées japonaises ou encore celle des longs métrages de cinéma d'animation. Avec 90% de ses visiteurs âgés de 25 à 64 ans et répartis à un niveau équivalent de près de 20% par tranche d'âge, cette collection s'adresse à un public très large et démontre l'existence d'un creuset d'échange intergénérationnel autour de cette thématique télévisuelle nippone.

Personnages issus de l'imagination des auteurs de mangas, insufflés à la vie par le crayon des animateurs, puis rehaussés par le pinceau des peintres et décorateurs, les documents originaux de production sont aujourd'hui reconnus et élevés au rang d'oeuvres d'art par les plus prestigieuses maisons de ventes aux enchères d'Europe. Ces oeuvres sont les témoins d'un patrimoine vivant industriel aujourd'hui révolu, héros de plastique et de papier mis en valeur de façon remarquable par des encadrements d'une qualité muséale.

Name: Antoine Schmitt Artist
Description:
Installation artist, Antoine Schmitt creates artworks in the form of objects, installations and situations to address the processes of movement in all of their modalities, and question their intrinsic conceptual problematics, of plastic, philosophical or social nature. Heir of kinetic art and cybernetic art, nourished by metaphysical science-fiction, he endlessly interrogates the dynamic interactions between human nature and the nature of reality. Originally programming engineer in human computer relations and artificial intelligence, he places the program, a contemporary artistic material and unique by its active quality, at the core of most of his artworks, to reveal and literally manipulate the forces at stake. Antoine Schmitt has also undertaken an articulation of this approach with more established artistic fields like music, dance, architecture, literature or cinema, and has collaborated with Franck Vigroux, Atau Tanaka, Vincent Epplay, Jean-Jacques Birgé, Delphine Doukhan, K.Danse, Patrice Belin, Don Nino, Cubenx, Alberto Sorbelli, Matthew Bourne, Hortense Gauthier… As theoretician, speaker and editor of the gratin.org portal, Antoine Schmitt explores the field of programmed art.

Antoine Schmitt is represented by Galerie Charlot (Paris) and gallery DAM Projects (Berlin)

Antoine Schmitt lives and works in Paris (FR).

Located in Newburgh, NY, it occupies 2,000 square feet on the ground level of the US workshop of Hysteric Glamour, an iconic Japanese streetwear company.

Name: John Davies Framing
Description:
From Rembrant to Picasso, from Manet to Monet, we have framed some of the most significant pieces in the history of art.

Our work isn’t just featured in museums and galleries worldwide; you’ll find them in elite private collections, palaces, and even on board superyachts.

Every frame is custom-made for the artwork it elevates. Our small and dedicated team have the highest attention to detail, with hundreds of years of elite craftsmanship experience between them.

Name: My Own Color Lab
Description: My Own Color Lab is a Fine Art  Exhibition printing lab specializing in photographic reproduction employing both digital and traditional printing methods in black and white and color. Not simply technicians, we are artisans who work hand in hand with artists, galleries and museums to deliver the highest quality prints up to mural size, that  represent their vision.  An integral part of this process is high resolution scanning, archiving, digital retouching and hand retouching. Originally conceived in 1978 as a color darkroom rental space, My Own Color Lab continues under the ownership of master printer Gerard Franciosa since 2002, to provide a place for photographers to print their work or have their work printed in collaboration with them. We bring a level of attention to the Print and the Artist that is unique in the industry.

Name: Solid Ground Movement
Description:
Solid Ground Movement is de eerste hiphopdansschool van Amsterdam en één van de pioniers in Amsterdam. Onze hiphopdansschool is gevestigd in Amsterdam Noord, in de bruisende Tolhuistuin. We bereiken jongeren met verschillende, culturele achtergronden en op verschillende dansniveau's. Jongeren, van de straat, leren bij ons om zich op een artistieke manier te uiten door middel van hiphopdans. Onze hiphopdansschool is geboren uit passie en biedt daarom een breed aanbod op het vlak van hiphop en breakdance, voor peuters, kinderen en (jong)volwassenen.

De artistieke leiding van onze hiphopdansschool is in de goede handen van Alida Dors en Bryan Druiventak. Alida en Bryan komen uit de hiphopscene en hebben van kinds af aan de ontwikkeling van hiphop meegedanst. Na jaren te hebben gedanst in de hiphopscene hebben zij zich gespecialiseerd in de theatrale kant van de hiphopdans en gaan ze vandaag de dag door het leven als makers. Hierdoor bieden zij nieuwe talenten de mogelijkheid om op het podium te schitteren, hun eigen kracht te ontdekken en deelbaar te maken met de community. Alida en Bryan deinsen er niet voor terug om in hun aanpak, innovatief en grensverleggend te werk te gaan. Doordat passie een verbindend thema is, is onze hiphopdansschool  een weerspiegeling van onze cultureel, diverse samenleving. We zijn er dan ook trots dat we het blijde nieuws mogen delen, met eenieder die gelooft in talent, dat het mastertraject Boost, een hiphop-talentontwikkelingsprogramma, in overweging wordt genomen voor de Code Culturele Diversiteit Award 2016! Neem hiervoor een kijkje op: http://codeculturelediversiteit.com/best-practice/master-traject-boost-solid-ground-movement/

Wij zijn er trots op dat onze hiphopdansschool zich hard maakt voor het overdragen van kennis van de hiphopcultuur, van generatie op generatie. Alles waarvoor wij ons inzetten, berust op een gezonde community feeling en kunnen wij alleen mogelijk maken dankzij de steun van onze samenwerkingspartners, onze dank, in verschillende delen van het land. We vinden het belangrijk dat talent een  podium krijgt en bieden de toplaag in de hiphopdanswereld inmiddels bijna een decennium carrieremogelijkheden. We geloven in de kracht van reflectie en zetten ons daarom structureel in voor onderzoek. Onze levensfilosofie luidt dan ook: You are never too young to dance!

Monochrome offers carefully selected essays, interviews, and features that aim to educate, inspire, and foster critical dialogue with contemporary artistic practices, with a special focus on Latin American art.

Name: CUYAHOGA VALLEY ART CENTER
Description:
About Cuyahoga Valley Art Center:
Nestled in the heart of historic downtown Cuyahoga Falls, the Cuyahoga Valley Art Center (CVAC) has enriched lives through the visual arts for over 90 years. Founded in 1934, CVAC is Summit County’s oldest nonprofit visual art organization and the only art center of its kind in the region. Our mission is to foster a greater understanding of the visual arts through education, exhibition, and community engagement — but what truly defines us is the creative community that brings this mission to life.

Each year, we welcome 1,300+ students to explore their creativity through 150+ classes and workshops in painting, drawing, ceramics, sculpture, and more. Our dedicated faculty and staff create a welcoming environment where learners of all ages — from 2nd graders to retirees — can discover and grow. Thanks to our Vibrant Palette Scholarship Fund, supported by the PNC Foundation and community donors, CVAC helps bridge the gap for students who might otherwise be unable to participate due to cost, expanding access to the joy of creative learning.

CVAC’s gallery exhibitions are a cornerstone of our work, attracting over 550 artists to submit work from across Ohio and beyond. These exhibitions draw 14,000+ visitors and serve as cultural touchstones where artists and art lovers connect, learn, and celebrate creativity together.

Collaboration is central to our mission. We partner with the Cuyahoga Falls Library, Cuyahoga Falls City Schools, and Akron Art Museum, and lead the CreativeCONNECT network, uniting local membership-based art groups— fostering collaboration and collective visibility for the visual arts.

At its core, CVAC is a home for creativity — a place where beginners take their first brushstroke, professionals refine their craft, and neighbors connect through the shared language of art. Whether you join a class, attend an exhibition, or support a scholarship, you become part of a vibrant legacy nearly a century in the making.

Belangrijk is dat de acteur/actrice tijdens de voorstelling zo dicht mogelijk op het publiek zit.
De voorstellingen hebben een maatschappelijk thema en worden veelal gebaseerd op boeken, liefst van Nederlandse bodem.

De eerste voorstelling van Solo Stories was LEEF!, te zien van januari t/m mei 2016. Van januari t/m mei 2017 tourde PAAZ door Nederland. Sinds januari 2018 brengt Solo Stories Ma, naar het gelijknamige boek van Hugo Borst. In het voorjaar van 2019 volgt Emma wil leven, de eerste voorstelling van Solo Stories die niet gebaseerd is op een boek, maar op een documentaire.

Name: The Little Theatre of Tuscarawas County
Description:
Formed in 1951, the first play, "Biography", was presented on October 9, 1951 at the former Union Opera House in New Philadelphia and subsequent productions were presented at the New Philadelphia Elks, Dover High School, Tuscora Park, and even a tent! During this time the membership realized the need to have its own building.

On September 9, 1961, the first shovel of earth was turned and construction began on the new theatre at 466 Carrie Ave. in New Philadelphia. Then on June 18, 1963, the new facility made its debut as The Little Theatre presented "The Miracle Worker" for eleven performances. This was the first time in the history of the organization that they were able to afford such a lengthy run. This milestone was reached only through the considerable amounts of money, building materials, and many hours of labor donated by members and friends of the theatre.

In 1990, a generous grant from the Reeves Foundation of Dover, Ohio funded the construction of an addition to the rear of the building, providing much-needed space for set construction and storage and wardrobe storage. Until this time, rehearsing performers tolerated the sawdust generated by on-stage set construction and the general congestion created by lack of storage space.

The air-conditioned structure includes 200 comfortable theatre seats; dressing rooms; facilities for prop storage, set building, wardrobe, and make-up; professional lighting; and a lobby with box office, refreshment counter and restroom facilities.

Over the years, The Little Theatre has had the privilege of presenting hundreds of productions, chosen to appeal to a wide variety of audiences. Productions range each season from musicals to dramas to comedies to children's productions. Everyone involved with The Little Theatre does so with no compensation, giving their time and talents to help present high-quality productions to the community.

Anlatı tiyatrosu, drama ve diksiyon atölyeleri, resim atölyeleri, enstrüman ve dans dersleri ile eğitimin yürütüldüğü kurumumuzda  amaç bir yandan estetik, empati, demokratik düşünme ve iletişim becerileri gelişmiş sanat takipçileri ve  bir yandan da sanatı meslek olarak düşünen bireyleri yetiştirmektir. Sanatbaz'ın genel amacı ise anlatı tiyatrosunun Türkiye’de tanınması, gelişmesini sağlamak ve uluslararası arenada da anlatı geleneğimizi temsil eden bir okul olmaktır.
Sanatbaz, öğrenci merkezli eğitim anlayışını öykü ve masalların büyülü dünyasıyla birleştirmektedir. Bu nedenle anlatı tiyatrosunu bir yöntem olarak bütün sanat eğitimi derslerinde kullanmaktadır. Böylece drama, piyano, keman, yan flüt, bale, modern dans, resim, heykel gibi derslerin bir yandan eğlenceli diğer yandan da kalıcı olması sağlanmaktadır.

Name: Lej Kunst
Description:
Lej Kunst er en service ydet fra ArtCompaz i Horsens.
Vi tilbyder virksomheder at få ny kunst hver 3. måned fra 175,-/md.

Der skal ikke meget til, før du med få virkemidler kan forbedre arbejdsmiljøet, motivere medarbejderne og forbedre din virksomheds image.  

Kunst i form af malerier og skulpturer, er i dag et af de væsentligste elementer, når det synlige arbejdsmiljø skal sikres. Samtidig signalerer god kunst på væggene, at din virksomhed er innovativ og seriøs.  

Tidligere var du nødt til at købe kunst, og regne med at du havde valgt rigtigt første gang, men nu får du et nyt koncept, hvor du - helt bogstaveligt - kan abonnere på kunst. Nemt, billigt og inspirerende!  

Se og oplev værkerne i din virksomhed, og køb når du har lyst. Der ydes 10% rabat på værkerne på den aktuelle udstilling til samarbejdende virksomheder og ansatte.

Vi har et nært samarbejde med en række danske kunstnere, der gør det muligt for os at tilbyde originale værker til din virksomhed på abonnement.  

Tænk på den signalværdi, der ligger i at have god originalkunst på væggene - og på den motivation, det giver både medarbejdere og kunder. Forestil dig så, at du kan udskifte kunstværkerne hvert kvartal. Og at du ikke skal købe - med mindre du gerne vil. Køb eller lej, og udnyt de attraktive afskrivningsregler, der er på kunst.  

Du skal ikke bruge tid og kræfter på at finde de helt rigtige kunstværker til din virksomhed - vi kommer til dig. Efter et besøg i din virksomhed, sørger vi for at tilbyde dig et bredt udvalg, der inspirerer og harmonerer med din indretning og image. Du skal ikke bruge tid og kræfter på at få udskiftet udstillingen, vi klarer det hele for dig.  

Det ER ingen kunst at forbedre både internt og eksternt image, når bare man ved hvordan. Det ved vi og vi overbeviser dig gerne med et uforpligtende forslag til, hvilken kunst, der passer bedst hos dig. Om  du så vil abonnere eller købe, eller begge dele, er helt op til dig.

Description: Pikes Peak Artist Collective is a 20 year old art school nestled in downtown Colorado Springs, CO. We are a boutique art school offering classes for children, teens and adults. Lessons are offered weekly and purchased as monthly memberships, as well as workshops taught by a wide variety of local artists in a broad array of mediums. All classes are small in size ranging from 1-17 people. Our studio space is outfitted with a gallery space, library, lounge area with a fire place, class area, clay class area, kitchen, bathroom, private parking and views of Pike Peak and the surrounding mountains. The culture of this small school is what makes it truly unique; we provide a supportive and dynamic community where one can feel comfortable and invigorated in the learning process! Name: Ed Dwight Studios, Inc.
Description:
Ed Dwight Sculpture Studios, Inc., located in Denver, CO operates a Studio, Gallery, and Foundry in a 25,000 sq.ft. facility. Since all the research for all the memorials is conducted in-house, the studio enjoys one of the larger historical based book, video and DVD collections in the country. The studio is composed of a Design Department, Creative Sculpture, Mold, Casting, Metal Finishing & Patina Departments. We also operate our own shipping department, as well as a wood fabrication area. The studio has the capacity to create & develop art projects using a full range of metals, plastics and other materials.

The art gallery is open for viewing & maintains a full range of Ed’s sculptures for your perusal. The studio is open for visits, preferably by appointment.

Name: Art Barcs
Description:
ArtBarcs is the place to discover art, and where artists get discovered. Simply click to "love" any work of art and we will suggest more art for you based on your "loves." Artists can upload their own artwork to get discovered by art lovers and those in the professional art world.

Over 500 of the world's leading living painters from five continents have come together to make it easier for you to discover and collect new art. Browse our extraordinary collection of images from this world artist's coalition, and when you see something that inspires you, click "love." Then, we'll show you more art you will love based on your preferences.

Already know what kind of art you love? Just send us images. Our amazing team of experts will help you expand your collection by locating that piece, or other artworks with similar attributes from artists worldwide. We are your private art concierge!

When you're ready to purchase art, we'll help you select pieces based on your budget and space, and make arrangements for flawless delivery. Best of all, the art prices are significantly lower than purchasing from galleries.

Name: WESTONARTS INC
Description: WESTONARTS INC is a company based out of PO BOX 1124, WESTON, Connecticut, United States.

Name: Smith & Brooks
Description:
As lovers of art, lovers of printing, lovers of canvas, lovers of fine art paper (yes, we love paper perhaps more than anything) we just needed to create. The feeling of filling a space with a beautiful image is something that drives us both.

We collaborate on our colour palettes, themes and compositions. We work out our subject matter based on how it will translate to different printing mediums and different printing sizes. The works will both make a statement and dominate on a large scale or bring quiet contemplation and calm focus on a small scale.

Our original works are then created by hand with acrylic paints, brushes and pens and professionally scanned using the latest in art reproduction technology. We print on only the finest of art papers, the environmentally friendly agave art paper and the highest quality, hand-stretched canvas. Our canvases are also available framed with Australian oak timber and all of our finished products are created through our local professional fine art printers.

Smith & Brooks are also dedicated to developing strong relationships with industry professionals as an art vendor and supplier. We welcome you to apply for a Trade account which will give you access to exclusive Trade pricing and allow you to enjoy other Trade benefits. So if you are an Interior Designer/Stylist, Decorator, Architect, Builder or Property Developer please be sure to get in contact with us.

–  Kerrin Smith & Jacky Brooks

Name: Coffee Art
Description:
Coffee Art – Creating art out of a cup of coffee

The Coffee Artists are Angel Sarkela-Saur and Andrew Saur, and they have been painting with coffee for over a decade which they call Coffee Art. Together, they developed a process and technique of painting with coffee. People have been using coffee as washes for sketches and other shading for some time, but this revolutionary process used to gain very dark hues is groundbreaking. The Coffee Artists take pride in their creations. They concentrate on creating fine art and the communication of their works using this new medium.

As a creative team, they have reinvented coffee as a painting medium – it’s not just an added gimmick, but rather a full-fledged commitment to a complete exploration of the medium’s potential. Coffee Art has grown into a comprehensive and professional studio practice shaped by their combined talents. Along the way, Coffee Art has garnered critical and popular acclaim, generating interest from a wide range of sources, and generating international invitations to show and discuss their work.

Their Coffee Art has received global attention and has attracted a strong following. The Coffee Artists have been featured in multiple news stories, documentaries and have been profiled on the Food Network’s “Unwrapped.” In addition to their media exposure, the Coffee Artists have exhibited work most recently in Milan and Athens, across the United States, Europe, and the United States Embassies in Malawi, Africa and Bogotá, Colombia.

Along with creating their Coffee Art paintings, they have produced animations of their paintings. On the animations page of their site, their Coffee Art paintings come to life in short film format.

The Coffee Artists invite you to peruse their online gallery for a more complete understanding of their works and to learn more about them.

So, pour yourself a cup of coffee, sit back, and enjoy their website at coffeeart.com.

Name: Vaudreuil-Soulanges Studio Tour

Name: Galleria Prencipe
Description:
The Galleria Prencipe, located in the Monteverde Vecchio quarter of Rome, is home to the Archivio Umberto Prencipe and to the Archivio dell'Ottocento Romano.
The gallery comprises a room dedicated to temporary art exhibitions, courses, and conferences, and two spaces which house the library, the collection of paintings, drawings and engravings, and the considerable archive material - handwritten pieces, press cuttings and photographs - present in Umberto Prencipe’s studio at the time of his death, as well as the documentary material belonging to the Archive.
As well as works by Prencipe, the collection also houses pieces by his circle, including Antonio Mancini, Vincenzo Migliaro, Giuseppe Casciaro, Attilio Pratella, Vincenzo Caprile, Francesco Vitalini, Antonio Antony De Wytt, and Ilario Ciaurro.
The spaces are decorated with objects and furniture from the artist’s studio, amongst which are a series of pieces of furniture decorated by Prencipe together with Ilario Ciaurro.
At the moment the library dedicated to art of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, engraving, and the history of art of Rome, is undergoing a period of cataloguing and reorganisation. The library contains over two thousand editions, numerous periodicals, and is growing thanks to donations. The Prencipe collection has merged with that of the Archive of Nineteenth Century Roman Art and the Library of Giuseppe Sacchi Lodispoto, a scholar of Rome. Further collections of books have been provided by the Tittoni family and by the library of the archeologist Giulio Quirino Giglioli. The catalogue will soon be available online.
Galleria Prencipe is at via Ludovico di Monreale 42 - 44, Rome.
The gallery is open for events and by appointment.

Name: Rare.Market
Description:
Not all compositions are equal. Not all art has the same level of intention and concept...and value appropriate to that intention and concept. We are not "the art police" but we are interested in building a safe haven for artists and buyers, where the art is meaningful and the value is rational and related to that meaning.

Therefore, our mission is to return value and power back to artists while allowing them to maximize profit. Rare.Market is a marketplace to enable that mission.

We do this by highly curating all works of art that come to our auction house.

Once the works are fully vetted and accepted, we facilitate the listing and simultaneous sale of physical artwork with its high resolution digital scan.

We use Non Fungible Tokens (NFTs) to track and validate the authenticity of the digital scan, and in doing so provide another channel for artists to distribute their work.

We are a new, different kind of auction company powered by a digital marketing agency and this approach—complete with our creative, technical, and marketing capabilities—ensures that we will get works of art noticed and sold.

We aim to make sure our artists can stay focused on the art they create, while we take care of the technical parts for them to make sure they can transact in new ways and new digital channels.

Name: CODA Gallery
Description:
Joy and passion surrounding fine art are hallmarks of CODA Gallery, an El Paseo landmark that's resonated with contemporary art collectors since 1987. Awaiting discovery inside CODA's magnificent gallery is a vast collection of paintings, sculpture, glass, and ceramic works by more than 150 established, mid-career, and emerging artists. 

Named Best Gallery in California
— American Art Awards, Huffington Post, April 2017

Name: Jolie Laide Gallery
Description:
Jolie Laide is an innovative forum for new art located in Center City Philadelphia at 224 N. Juniper St. presenting work by largely unseen or early career artists exhibiting in the US and abroad. As an exhibition space, we advocate progress, new ideas and trends in contemporary art by actively pursuing young artists and provoking new work in an effort to support innovation in contemporary art.  

We are dedicated to creating a supportive environment for new works through the promotion of conceptual dialogue. Exhibitions will be supplemented with written works by a gifted group of art thinkers whom have also contributed to Beautiful/Decay, Juxtapoz and the Brooklyn Rail. These components will be published on our website as part of the online catalogue that will accompany each show. Our stimulating programming includes two weeks of performances and site-specific installations in our Project Space during the Philadelphia Fringe Festival in September, followed by a guest curated show in our Main Gallery by artist Wendy White in November.

We look forward to an exciting first year marked by an extremely talented group of artists and a stunning, newly renovated 2,000 sq. ft. space comprised of two ground floor galleries flanking Juniper Street in the burgeoning industrial blocks east of Broad Street. The Main Gallery is a 1,000 sq. ft. raw space with concrete floors and 15ft tall brick walls with an adjoining 300 sq. feet of white-walled, exhibition space with 10 ft. ceilings. Across the street is the 700 sq. ft. Project Space primed for site-specific installations, film viewing, public talks and artist lectures.

Name: Pessima
Description:
PESSIMA is a Transfeminist Art Collective that aims to promote and support Trans* & Non Binary emerging artists and projects.

The name comes from the Italian word for "terrible", "the worst".
Terms that have always defined the trans community in the public eye, and labeled this minority as inferior and not worthy.
We make fun of it, and build pride out of their ignorance.

Today we show the potential of these people through the beauty and power of their arts.

Name: CK Consultancy
Description:
Voor enkele verzekeraars en makelaars heb ik verschillende polisvoorwaarden herschreven. Mijn uitgangspunten daarvoor zijn: polisvoorwaarden moeten eenvoudig toegankelijk, leesbaar en begrijpelijk zijn voor de klant, verzekerde en verzekeringnemer. 

Noot: Jip-en-Janneke taal gebruiken is een grove onderschatting van de intelligentie van de doorsnee verzekerden /verzekeringnemers! 

Een makelaar die de door mij herschreven polisvoorwaarden gebruikte, ontving daarop ongevraagd deze reactie van zijn klant: “Ik heb nog nooit zulke duidelijke polisvoorwaarden gezien.” 

Polisvoorwaarden die aan de genoemde criteria voldoen zijn niet alleen een verkoopargument. Het voorkomt ook 1) vragen = arbeidsbesparing 2) ongewenste discussies in geval van schade. = arbeidsbesparing respectievelijk besparing op externe kosten. 

Basically I have been a property insurance broker. Manager of the property departement of ABN AMRO. Since 1988 I became an insurance broker for museums, exhibition organisers. I was employed by ABN AMRO and Aon.

I have been chair of various commiitees in the Dutch insurance market. Some of these committees were focussed on rewriting policywording for the co-insurance market.

CK Consultancy (re-)wrote the policywording for several insurers and brokers. An essential target was making the wording accessible, readable and understandable for clients; a real sellingpoint.

Name: Nicholas Grindley LLC
Description:
Nicholas Grindley has been dealing and researching Chinese art with particular interest in furniture and works of art for over 40 years. During most of this time he has conducted his business as a dealer, firstly as Nicholas Grindley Works of Art Ltd in the UK and then Nicholas Grindley LLC which was formed in the USA in 2018. Since 1998 he has published two catalogues a year, exhibited in London and New York; many works from these exhibitions and his other dealing activities are in museums and private collections through-out the world. In 1996 he contributed to the catalogue of the Mimi and Raymond Hung Collection of Chinese Furniture and in 1999 he co-wrote with Robert Jacobsen the catalogue of Classical Chinese Furniture in the Minneapolis Institute of Arts. In 2004 he wrote the catalogue for the collection of Dr Ignazio Vok that was exhibited at the Museum fur Ostasiatische Kunst in Cologne. In 2007 he contributed the entry for Chinese Furniture for ‘The Seventy Wonders of China’ published by Thames & Hudson. He is asked by museums in the US and Europe to advise on the acquisition, disposal, cataloging and re-attribution of Chinese furniture in their collections. He continues to deal privatley in Chinese art in New York and London. 

Nicholas Grindley has been assisted by Rebecca Gardner since 1995. Rebecca was originally a Director of Nicholas Grindley Works of Art Ltd, but now works alongside Nicholas Grindley as a freelance Art Registrar and Manager.

Name: ANHAD
Description:
La Asociación Nacional de Historiadores de Arte y Diseño (ANHAD) es una asociación sin ánimo de lucro constituida en Febrero de 2010 con la firme vocación de servir al estudio, desarrollo y difusión del Arte y el Diseño como disciplinas complementarias al tiempo que independientes. Analizar y comprender  lo constitutivo de cada una de ellas  es nuestro primer objetivo.

Para difundir el Arte y el Diseño más allá del contexto especializado o universitario hemos creado la revista digital ANHAD, de periodicidad bimestral y que podéis consultar de forma gratuita en esta web.

La promoción de artistas contemporáneos es otra de nuestras prioridades, por lo que estaremos encantados de recibir y mostrar vuestras propuestas artísticas.

Tampoco nos olvidamos de los estudiantes y jóvenes investigadores, a los que animamos a colaborar en nuestra revista a través de artículos de investigación, de opinión, críticas a exposiciones o recensiones a libros.

Name: Conservart snc di Chiara Canevara e Davide Cesari
Description:
La Società di Restauro ConservArt s.n.c. nasce nel 2001 a Tavazzano con Villavesco (Lodi) dall’unione delle pluriennali esperienze professionali dei due soci titolari: dr.ssa Chiara Canevara e dr. Davide Cesari. 
Siamo specializzati nel restauro conservativo ed estetico di opere d’arte antiche e contemporanee. La nostra grande passione per l’arte e per tutte le sue varie forme di espressione ci guida a intervenire con il massimo rispetto e responsabilità sull’opera d’arte al fine di garantire la sua corretta conservazione nel corso del tempo. La nostra esperienza lavorativa si è sempre accompagnata a corsi d’aggiornamento e alla volontà di approfondire lo studio storico artistico delle opere su cui interveniamo. 
Lavoriamo per enti pubblici (Soprintendenze, Direzioni Regionali, Province, Comuni), società private, curie, parrocchie, associazioni, privati cittadini. 
Nel 2011 abbiamo ottenuto la certificazione SOA, cat. OS2, class. 1.

Name: ECoS (Espacio Común Sur)
Description: ECoS (Espacio Común Sur) es un espacio donde el aprendizaje artístico se transforma en acción. Ofrecemos cursos, talleres y charlas para explorar el arte contemporáneo, conectándote con una comunidad creativa. Aquí no solo aprendes, sino que también puedes adquirir obras de artistas locales, apoyando su trabajo mientras enriqueces tu colección personal.

Name: Collectif GRAPHITES
Description:
Nous sommes deux architectes, une artiste plasticienne et une designeuse d'espace inspirées par l’espace, les mouvements, les couleurs, la vie.

Nous travaillons collectivement sur des questions qui se rapportent aux problématiques d’appropriation et d’expérience de l’espace. Nous concevons et créons des outils expérimentaux qui permettent de questionner nos rapports à l’espace et à son utilisation.

Name: University of Auckland Photography Society (Photosoc)
Description:
Established in 1960s, Auckland University Photographic Society is one of the longest-running clubs at university. Photosoc aims to provide opportunities and support for its members to grow their passion for photography in both amateur and professional contexts. Our goal, backed by over 50 years of experience, is to grow better photographers and more of them. PhotoSoc welcomes all students with an interest in photography, whether they are absolute beginners or advanced professionals. 

You can also email us:
President: photosoc.president@gmail.com
Membership Manager: photosoc.membership@gmail.com
Treasurer: photosoc.finance@gmail.com

What we do… 

*General Meetings - Take a break, get together, and mix & mingle with other members, sponsors, and committee and find out about upcoming events. 

*Field Trips - Get to know other members whilst adding to your photography experience. Some of the places we’ve been to include Tongariro National Park, Cape Reinga, Hamilton Gardens, the Auckland Fire Station and Devonport. 

*Workshops – PhotoSoc offers workshops for all levels; from the complete beginner to the advanced photographer. In the past we have run camera basics, studio lighting, astrophotography, Photoshop tips & tricks, pinhole photography and more. 

*Exhibitions - A golden opportunity for members to display their work, we have exhibitions every semester and in the past been included as part of the Auckland Festival of Photography. 

*Equipment & Resources – PhotoSoc has access to a high quality film scanner for scanning of 120 and 135 negatives. We also have access to a Canon 5DM2 for members to hire out, as well as some flashes for any camera system. Film shooters are catered to as well, with film camera hire and film sales. Come to one of our office hours to try something out!

Name: Markstadt
Description:
Innovating Brand Experiences | Your Partner in Audio-Visual Excellence

With over 10 years of expertise in the audio-video production industry, our studio has become a trusted partner for local and international brands looking to deliver impactful creative solutions. We specialize in crafting tailored content that elevates brand storytelling across multiple platforms, combining technical precision with a deep understanding of audience engagement.

3 years ago, we expanded our portfolio by launching a game-changing audio advertising service. This innovative solution goes beyond personalized radio, transforming in-store environments into immersive brand experiences. By integrating custom playlists, dynamic messaging, and branded audio content, we help our partners create emotional connections with their customers and amplify their brand identity at the point of sale.

Our approach is rooted in collaboration, innovation, and ensuring that every project exceeds expectations. Whether it’s audio branding, video production, or integrated campaigns, we’re here to make your brand stand out.

Having attended and graduated in graphic design at George Brown College of Applied Arts, his background in art is extensive and has been painting murals professionally since 1997. Painting murals in a wide variety of interior/exterior and automotive surfaces, his work covers a broad range of themes and he is always looking to expand his professional portfolio.
