Name: Keramiek Atelier Antoinette
Description:
Mijn atelier heet Keramiek Atelier Antoinette. Hier geef ik cursussen en workshops. Meer informatie is te vinden op de website www.ilse.kijkt.nl.

Workshop keramiek - ook geschikt als teamuitje
Een mooie schaal of kom maken, dat kan jij ook! Tijdens de keramiek workshops leer ik het je!
Ook leuk in combinatie met een Turks buffet.

Keramiek Cursus - Rakustoken
Keramieken omdat je tot rust wilt komen. De klei doet zijn werk. Je bent gewoon lekker ontspannen aan het boetseren. De klei zorgt ervoor dat je aardt en werkt meditatief/rustgevend. Bij mij mag u in uw eigen tempo bezig zijn. 

Boetseren is heerlijk om te doen en de cursus is gezellig. In een groep van maximaal 6 personen werk je in het atelier. Tijdens de cursus wordt er geboetseerd met klei ook wel handvormen genoemd. Ieder cursusblok heb ik een planning met verschillende opdrachten. Zo leer je verschillende technieken. Vrij werken mag ook.
Klei is een heerlijk materiaal en werkt langzaam. Dat is voor sommigen wennen. Als er voldoende werkstukken zijn, stook ik die in de oven. Altijd spannend. Daarna ga je glazuren. Dat is heel gek als je dat voor het eerst doet. De kleuren die je op de gebakken klei aanbrengt zijn anders dan de kleuren, zoals ze worden na het stoken. En als je denkt dat je weet hoe het gaat worden, kan het ook weer anders uitvallen. Door al deze verrassingen blijf ik keramiek zo leuk vinden. In de keramiek-keuken zijn aanwezig: Witte steengoedklei, glazuren, oxides, pigmenten, bodystain, engobes. In het voorjaar gaan we ook altijd Rakustoken.

En wie ben ik?
Mijn naam is Ilse Antoinette Scholten en ik ben keramiste/beeldend kunstenares in Enschede. Lid van de Nederlandse Vakgroep Keramisten.

The collective consists of an art gallery, the resident artists in the studios on the premises, and a wide-ranging community of exhibiting artists, from local to national, and from as far away as Greece and Italy.

The mission of HIVE artspace is to inspire, motivate and support our artists, to work together to bring independent art into a gallery setting, and to actively participate in, cultivate and nurture the growing creative community within York City.

Name: Fisch Bowl, Inc.
Description:
Fisch Bowl, Inc., is a 501(c)(3) organization that endeavors to promote the arts and offer a wide variety of cultural experiences and resources to the public.  At this time, they present all their programming in the Fisch Haus, a 21,000 s.f. warehouse owned and operated by a five-member artist’s collaborative.  Fisch Haus has been active since 1990;  for many years, self-producing annual large-scale exhibitions for each of its resident artists.  However, limited by a restricted budget and schedule, the full-time artists were not able to open their facility as often or extensively as they wished, so they incorporated as Fisch Bowl, Inc. in June of 2004 and received their not-for-profit status in March of 2005.  Fisch Bowl’s multi-disciplinary event roster focuses on fine art installations, but is constantly expanding to include community art events ranging from performance art and new media, to lectures, classes, benefits, readings, plays, and music festivals; offered free of charge or admission by donation, and open to all ages.  

Fisch Bowl, Inc. (EIN 20-1591210) Mission Statement:
To promote the arts and offer a wide variety of cultural experiences to the public, regardless of any individual’s ability to pay.

Name: Jade Kerste Art Mediation
Description:
Ik denk dat kunst iedereen iets te bieden heeft. Daarom help ik beeldend kunstinstellingen en kunstenaars om zich meer op het publiek te richten, zodat ze nieuwe bezoekers gaan aantrekken en ze publiek een zo goed mogelijke ervaring met kunst kunnen bieden.

Dat doe ik door middel van publiekssessies, waarbij we vanuit de missie van je kunstorganisatie nadenken over het bereiken van nieuw publiek. Daarnaast geef ik art mediation rondleidingen en schrijf ik publieksgerichte teksten. Toegankelijk, maar niet makkelijk. Hoe kunnen we de ervaring van het publiek verbeteren? Hoe trekken we nieuwe bezoekers aan? Hoe kunnen we kunst het beste naar het publiek overbrengen? Dat zijn vragen waar ik me mee bezig houd.

Eens in de twee weken stuur ik mijn mail Publieksteksten, over alles wat met kunst en publiek te maken heeft. Stuur me een bericht om deze ook te ontvangen.

Jade in het kort:
Gestructureerd / Praktisch / Helder / Ambitieus / Rustig / Verantwoordelijk / Grappig

www.jadekerste.nl

Name: Traqueur de Lumières
Description:
Created in 2001, Traqueur de Lumieres focuses mainly on Abstract Photography of the Belgian artist Yves Ullens.

Yves Ullens sees himself as a permanent searcher and works to expand the frontiers of photography by mixing it with other media:

. In 2008, he mixed his photographies with painting with the wellknown belgian painter Pascal Courcelles.

. In 2010, on the basis of his Coloured Constallation #1 work, he designed an artistic evening & cocktail dress with the Argentinian stylist Debora Velasquez.

. In 2011, he created a monumental photographic installation for the BKCP Bank headquarters in Brussels (12,9m (height) x 7,9m (width) of 88 elements).

. in 2013, he designed "Délivrance", his first monumental sculpture for the outside.

. In 2014, he mixed photography with the comic strip techniques in his creation for the W.Z.C. Mariahuis in Gavere (Belgium). He also realized "Crescend'O", the largest Photosculpture installation in ChromaLuxe in the world for the Beobank Headquarters (Brussels).

. In 2015, he realized "Triple Harmony" his first multimedia installation (photo + video) for the Board of Directors room the RTL Group (Brussels).

He is expected to launch in September his very first design object (low tables).

Name: Donna B Fine Art
Description:
Contemporary equestrian and abstractive artist.
I combine mediums in unique and regenerative ways. My work exhibits the beauty, nature, and healing power of the inspirational horse.
What gives my work its edge is a deep, personal familiarity with the physical horse, and an expansive sense that the movement is bigger than what you see.
As I have learned, it is not horses that I paint; I paint the way they make me feel. 
Commission and Designer Inquiries welcome.

Name: The Oriental Ceramic Society of Hong Kong 香港東方陶瓷學會 (Est. 1974)
Description:
The Oriental Ceramic Society of Hong Kong 香港東方陶瓷學會 was established in 1974 by a group of collectors and enthusiasts with a shared interest in Asian art and culture. It is dedicated to the open exchange of ideas and information between scholars, collectors and the public through a series of lectures, guided exhibition viewings, exclusive collection visits, popular study tours, and masterclasses.

The OCS has organised a number of major exhibitions during its history and published catalogues to accompany these exhibitions:
- Jingdezhen Wares: The Yuan Evolution (1984)
- Art From the Scholar’s Studio (1986)
- Ancient Chinese and Ordos Bronzes (1990)
- Two Thousand Years of Chinese Lacquer (1993)
- Heavens’ Embroidered Cloths: One Thousand Years of Chinese Textiles (1995)
- The Passion of Collecting (1999)
- Art and Imitation in China (2006)
- The Dragon in Chinese Art (2012)

The Oriental Ceramic Society of Hong Kong is an independent non-profit organisation that receives no external funding. Your membership, the generosity of our speakers, patrons and supporters allows us to continue our vibrant programme of lectures, masterclasses, and special collector events. You can support us by joining as a member, or by supporting individual events or projects. Your generosity can make a difference, please contact us if you would like to support our work:
Email: ocs@orientalceramics.org.hk, using ‘support’ in the subject heading
Telephone: +852 6140 6742

The Oriental Ceramic Society of Hong Kong was incorporated as a company limited by guarantee in 1992 and is recognised as a charitable institution by the Inland Revenue Department with the result that donations to the OCS are deductible for Hong Kong tax purposes.

Name: Rare Furniture
Description:
The business
Rare Furniture specialises in identifying ancient Chinese furniture and objects in precious woods, species identification, and huanghuali / zitan / hongmu / et al conservation.
Our commitment to purist technique reflects the zenith of the cabinetmakers art. We provide services such as pre-auction appraisals, condition reports, personal and gallery collection maintenance, exhibition preparation together with the on-site repair of Chinese precious hardwood furniture and objects.

The clients
Clients include Bonhams, The London BADA, AAIL and 1st Dibs trade; various international premier auctioneers; and clients ranging from Europe to South Africa and Singapore.

The team
We have an excellent understanding of Chinese literati furniture and of the Global Chinese Art Auction Market in general, in addition to VoC trade route furniture. Owing to our extensive expertise in restoration, identification and conservation of precious Chinese and Asian tropical hardwoods (predominantly huanghuali , zitan, hongmu, padouk) coupled with extensive botanic and historic research on the topic and a personal timber sample library, we are uniquely qualified to identify between the various exotic hardwood species used in the construction of Ming and Qing imperial and scholarly furniture and works of art. 

The owner
With nearly thirty years of experience in the antiques industry as a trade supplier, he is among a handful of global Chinese period furniture gurus, is (among others) Bonhams Chinese precious hardwood consultant and conservator, and conducts appraisals throughout the British Isles & Europe.

" I am passionate about minimalism and the inherent quality of Daoist purism – Less is more” Kirkwood Paterson

The ethos:
A dichotomy lies at the heart of studies on Chinese furniture. On the one hand there is a lack of genuine Chinese archaic literature on the subject, owing in no small part to Chairman Mao's 'destruction of symbols of opulence' policy, which resulted in the annihilation of ancient texts, forestry records, timber xyloriums, and world of knowledge wantonly burned on street bonfires during one of the bloodiest eras in Chinese history. On the other hand (popularised by Gustav Ecke's wartime publication 'Chinese Domestic Furniture') an intense aesthetic appreciation in the west for hardwood furniture thought to date to the late Ming dynasty emerged in the post war Euro/American Zeitgeist. Various publications emerged during this time, many of which expounded the Taoist aesthetic andpurity of form, and all bar one (R H Ellsworth) dedicated no more than one page to the species employed in the construction of these rare works of art.
This is a pattern that continues in publications of Chinese works of art in precious woods to this day.

Description:

Our goal is to reimagine how to make and market art. In other words, we are striving to create a new business model and, in the process, build an art brand recognized around the world.

Name: Waguespack Industries
Description:
Fine Art Paintings, Art & Design, Photography, & Home Goods - Changing the landscape of Art Collecting one day at a time

John Waguespack Industries provides fine art (fusing of different mediums, oil, acrylic, ink, and industrial design) to galleries and collectors worldwide.  Waguespack  creates abstract paintings incorporating pop-art icons to address political issues of today and the '70s, figurative, abstract striped images, and pop culture.  Waguespack is among the 21st Century artists who are strongly influenced by social media and the demands of young collectors to create art they can relate to.

The home goods line Salvador's Garden was founded by John Finch and John Waguespack in June 2014.  After successfully funding a Kickstarter crowdfunding campaign, the team raised $26,000 to develop their First collection and delivered orders to over 200 new customers within 30 days.  The inspiration for the images comes from the creative mind of John Waguespack, a San Francisco Artist.  Waguespack was inspired by a series of Dali paintings he used to create the garden experience on this amazing fabric - made of 100% recycled plastic bottles. Everything in this garden, the birds, flowers, animals, are transformations of the original portrait. The beautiful snow white fabrics reminded Waguespack of that original painting and thought it would be the perfect medium on which to share this series.  By removing these plastic bottles from our landfills we are helping to keep our planet from turning into a waste dump.  This process takes what would be trash and upcycles it into something useful and beautiful.

Name: BronzeCo
Description:
The sculptures of BronzeCo gallery make for beautiful, well-crafted, substantial, yet affordable conversational pieces and artwork. The collection is limited, diverse, and compiled of ever so unique pieces. 

What even further separates BronzeCo from other sculpture galleries, is the ability for clientele to collaborate with the BronzeCo team to create their own custom piece of bronze sculpture. BronzeCo's mission is to inspire, and be inspired by you, and bring your personal artistic vision to reality. 

Currently we manage both an online storefront, and a showroom within the prestigious ddc (domus design collection), in the heart of New York City, on 34th and Madison Avenue. We also have an Interior Designer on staff to assist in selecting pieces.

Name: Maha Art
Description:
At our online art school, we bring the ancient wisdom of Indian traditional art to the modern world. Led by Maha, our esteemed faculty member with over 16 years of experience and a follower base of 75,000 on Instagram, we specialize in teaching Shilpashastra — the intricate and spiritual art form deeply rooted in Indian heritage.

Why Choose Us? Our unique pedagogy, based on the Taalamaana Paddati, offers a structured approach to mastering Shilpashastra. Whether you're a beginner eager to start your journey or an artist looking to deepen your skills, we have something for everyone:

7-Month Course for Beginners: Lay a strong foundation in the principles and techniques of Shilpashastra.
Advanced Course: Elevate your artistry with in-depth studies and practice.
Special Workshops: Learn to draw full deities and bring divine figures to life with precision and grace.
Meet Maha With a Bachelor’s in Fine Arts from Chitra Kala Parishat, Bangalore, and a Master’s in Fine Arts from Bangalore University, Maha’s passion and expertise shine through in every lesson. Her friendly and spiritually enriching teaching style makes learning both enjoyable and deeply rewarding.

Join Our Community Visit our website to see the inspiring testimonials and incredible results achieved by our students. Embark on a transformative journey of art and spirituality today!

Name: Bureau Des Arts ESIEA
Description:
Le Bureau des Arts est la 3e plus grande association de l’ESIEA. Chaque année, et ce depuis 2001, une nouvelle liste est élue au mois de mars pour une durée d’un an. Le but de cette association est de promouvoir l’art et la culture au sein de notre école d’ingénieurs. La musique, le dessin, la danse, le théâtre, la cuisine et la photographie y sont représentés à travers différents pôles qui organisent des évènements comme des cours de danse, karaokés ou afterworks inter-associatifs. 
Cette année, le BDA Olympe est composé de 11 membres : les responsables de pôles et le bureau général, tous bien décidés à faire de ce BDA une association inoubliable. 
Cette année, plusieurs nouveaux pôles ont vu le jour : le pôle magie, créer pour impressionner et divertir les étudiants, le pôle photo chargé d’organiser des concours ainsi que d’immortaliser tous les évènements de l’école ainsi que le pôle sponsor, chargé de faire le lien entre le BDA et les entreprises.

Name: AndreGACKO
Description:
Avec assurance et dextérité, le sujet jaillit de ses mains. Vous verrez apparaître des animaux, comme un dragon, un cheval, un lion, un rapace, un chien, un taureau.. mais aussi, des personnages comme Don Quichotte, Sancho Pança, Arthur Rimbaud, un matador, un picador, des artistes de cirque, des héros de la mythologie grecque, des lutteurs sénégalais, les massais, le Père Noe....
Vous verrez précisément les muscles saillants, l'œil, les cheveux, la crinière flottant au vent, les sabots, les ergots, les serres crochues, une multitude de détails qui donnent vie à des sculptures toujours en mouvement et animent l'œuvre de l'artiste.
Autodidacte, son centre d'intérêt est porté vers une considération de l'homme.

À l'aide de papier de cahier d'écolier, de papier blanc, de papier aluminium, il froisse, triture, chiffonne le matériau jusqu'à lui donner la forme désirée et s'inscrit ainsi dans le mouvement de la figuration narrative. Le résultat obtenu révèle une sculpture en papier de 10 centimètres à 2 mètres de haut offrant une pièce unique pleine de poésie.
Le papier d'un grammage spécial, travaillé de façon sauvage, non conformiste en plus d'une résine solidifiant, offre la résistance nécessaire à la bonne tenue de la sculpture. dans le temps
A 6 ans, au jardin d'enfants de Dakar, GACKO, dès ses premières sensations, utilise la pâte à modeler, la résine, le fil de fer, l'argile pour reproduire des animaux.
Nourri d'émissions télévisuelles, il aime à construire des vaisseaux, des personnages fantastiques qui le guideront ensuite vers le matériau à sa disposition: le papier, maintenant sa matière de prédilection.
Ainsi, GACKO valorise un matériau quotidien, banal et nous invite à voir par un prisme nouveau le papier qui entre ses mains devient une oeuvre.
Il expose sur divers thèmes, dans de nombreux lieux et de salon international d'art contemporain.

Name: Mosi-O-Tunya Gallery
Description:
Located in Holland Park, London, the Mosi-O-Tunya Gallery showcases bold and colourful works by artists from Zimbabwe, Zambia, Mozambique and South Africa.

We currently have a selection of pieces from the Wulfsohn Collection available to buy with proceeds from sales used to support emerging African artists.

Mosi-O-Tunya, or “the smoke that thunders”, is the African tribal name for the Victoria Falls on the Zambezi River, the boundary between Zambia and Zimbabwe from where most of the permanent collection comes and where the Gallery's founders, the Wulfsohn Family lived.

Name: Biela Kocka
Description:

 Ocenenie je udeľované za edičné, prezentačné, edukačné, teoretické, či iné odborné projekty v oblasti galerijnej práce na Slovensku. Zámerom je uznanie odbornej práce, motivácia galerijných pracovníkov a spolupracovníkov, popularizácia galerijných aktivít. 
 Nomináciu projektov spúšťame už čoskoro. Sledujte naše sociálne siete a web, kde budeme zverejňovať všetky potrebné informácie.

Name: Ordained Canvas
Description: rdained Canvas is a Louisiana Veteran-owned Art Studio that will cater to children who have a Mental Health diagnosis. However, it will be available to everyone. Our mission is to help children overcome mental & emotional trauma through art while our motto is Using Art to bring mental & emotional healing to the soul. We will offer prophetic art classes for adults, art activities for children, mental health events, and more beginning in Spring  2023. There will be an emphasis on helping children ages 7 to 14 overcome mental roadblocks focusing on those who are autistic and suffer from selective mutism, anxiety disorders, OCD, and PTSD.

Name: Zen Art & Design
Description:
With our hand designed, wooden jigsaw puzzle product lines, our mission is to make unique, inspiring, heirloom quality, artisanal wooden jigsaw puzzles at affordable, mainstream prices using eco-friendly materials.  From raw materials to retail packaging and beyond, we strive to be pioneers in eco-friendly craftsmanship and artistic creativity while pricing our puzzles at competitive prices.

Figural pieces, known as whimsical pieces (or “Whimsies”) in England, are one of the major features that differentiate the premium, wooden jigsaw puzzle from the die-cut, cardboard puzzle.  They were included in wooden puzzles cut in the early 1900’s and throughout the 20th century, but we take them to another level–a level no one but the insatiably curious would ever perceive unless we blog about it.  For instance, the Clown Fish is immune to the poison of the anemone in which it lives; in determining the figural pieces for our Chocolate Clownfish Zen and Peapod Puzzles, we based our research for the figural pieces on that fact, and, after some rather specific and lengthy research, we decided the figural pieces for the puzzle would all be fish that were also immune to the poison of the anemone.

We contribute a portion of profits from Zen Puzzles to benefit environmental causes, and a portion of profits from Peapod Puzzles to benefit children's health programs, but, perhaps more importantly, an inspiring part of our mission (for us, at least) is to collaborate with non-profits and environmental causes through creating special edition puzzles and other products dedicated to fundraising for those causes.  We’ve also worked hard to make our products and their packaging as eco-friendly as possible and to use raw goods made entirely in the USA, and are proud that our Zen Puzzles are eco-friendly and made entirely in the USA!

Name: MINERVA CCAE
Description: MINERVA est le représentant officiel de l’art et de la culture au sein de l’ESITH. Il a pour mission principale de renforcer et enrichir le côté artistique et culturel chez les étudiants vu son importance pour le développement du côté créatif et innovant. Tout ceci se fera à travers des activités para universitaire, événements, formations et distractions artistiques et culturelles, qui permettent aux étudiants non seulement de se distraire et s’épanouir mais aussi améliorer leurs relationnelles et leurs gestion de projets.

Name: Artmoshai
Description:
The name Artmoshai is derived from the word "Art" and "Moshai". The word "Moshai" is a Bengali term for addressing someone with honor and respect. Artmoshai was founded by creative art enthusiast and entrepreneur Srijan Mandal, who desired of creating a business that fused traditional art with a blend of technology and interactive experimentation.

Artmoshai was conceived to create an online platform for artists from around the world from different backgrounds, culture, styles to unite and have their works displayed and sold to the global audience.

Description:
EXPERTISE

Name: Elder Music Studio
Description:
Welcome to Elder Music Studio! We offer lessons for beginners in piano, saxophone, and guitar. Online lessons are available for K-12th grade students located anywhere within the United States. In-person lessons are also available in our West Point, VA location. 

Elder Music Studio is a place for new musicians. We have a love of children, music, life, and a passion for sharing what we've learned in our own musical journeys. Our goal is to help new musicians gain an understanding of the musical language and foster a love of bringing the notes and the instrument to life.

Name: Tsukiyo Art

Tsukiyo literally translates to 'Moonlit' in Japanese.

Name: ABRI Paris
Description:
ABRI, c'est l'art et la nouvelle manière.

Nous avons imaginé ABRI parce que nous sommes convaincus que le fait d'acheter une œuvre d'art peut sortir du domaine de l'invraisemblable. En partenariats avec des artistes, des collectionneurs et des marchands, nous avons construit une sélection d'œuvres que nous vous proposons de louer pendant trois à six mois, sans vous engager.

Apprivoisez, ravisez-vous, échangez, accrochez une nouvelle œuvre. Si vous ne pouvez plus vous en séparer, nous indexons le prix de vente sur la durée de la location.

Notre ambition : réussir à alléger l'engagement que peut représenter le fait d'acheter de l'art - symboliquement et financièrement - pour que collectionner cesse d'être quelque chose qui n'arrive qu'aux autres.

Description: Located in Marion, IA, Philip's Diamond Shop is a recognized expert in the field of CAD/CAM jewelry design and manufacture, specializing in custom design for our clients around the world. Philip's Diamond Shop also has a full range of jewelry in our location in Marion, IA. From the smallest repair to custom hand engraving to custom design and manufacturing, it all happens right here at Philip's Diamond Shop.

Name: Amis de la Galerie Pictura, Agapes
Description:
AGAPES | Amis de la Galerie Pictura
Organismes d'intérêt général à caractère culturel
Conseil d’administration : 3 chefs d’entreprise (GE, PME, TPE) et 1 directeur artistique, bénévoles

Créée en 2009 et jusqu'en 2014, l’association a pour mission principale de promouvoir et développer la création contemporaine, en particulier de valoriser la programmation artistique présentée à la Galerie Pictura (en convention avec la Ville de Cesson-Sévigné). 
Lieu d’exposition de la scène artistique contemporaine dédié à l’expression picturale figurative, les artistes Robert Combas, Marc Desgranchamps, Philippe Hortala, et Philippe Mayaux notamment, auront présentés leur travail. 

Le coeur d'activité de l'association est de soulèver les financements et fédèrer les partenariats pour promouvoir ses missions auprès de différents publics: édition d'art et soutien à la production d'art.

Dès 2015, l'association vole de ses propres ailes et ouvre également un pôle de résidences et d'ateliers artistiques. 

Suivez les projets: http://ailesdecaius.fr/

Name: Marie-Claude Désilets
Description: Mon travail s'exprime surtout en peinture, dessin et céramique présentement.  Je suis fascinée par le comportement humain, du pouvoir de l'imaginaire qui est souvent accompagné d'un narratif.  Les textures me parlent beaucoup et complémentent mes oeuvres.

Name: Universal Zulu Nation Belgian Chapter
Description:
L'Universal Zulu Nation est une organisation fondée le 12 Novembre 1973 étroitement liée à la naissance de la Culture Hip Hop en 1974.
 
Le Chapter belge de l'UZN est un groupe d'amis officelement actif dans le mouvement depuis 1994.
Pendant les 20 dernières années, nous avons organisé un grand nombre d'événements variés tels que des concerts, des festivals, des conférences, des débats et des Battles.
 
Nous avons aussi développé Street Knowledge TV & Radio qui sont les principaux médias Hip Hop de Belgique reconnu dans le monde entier.
 
A présent, nous avons décidé de proposer nos services à tous ceux qui désirent développer des concepts ayant un lien avec la Culture Hip Hop et sa philosophie.

Nous sommes tous animés par le même désir de transmettre des valeurs auxquelles nous croyons:

L'intégrité : Afin de surmonter n'importe quel défi, nous avons regroupé les bonnes personnes avec un véritable esprit d'équipe et qui feront le job.
Nous sommes totalement investi dans notre travail dans le but d'apporter un succès exceptionnel à nos clients, et ce en visant l'excellence stratégique et opérationnelle dans tout ce que nous faisons.

La créativité : Nous fournissons, créons et développons des idées et concepts novateurs qui peuvent donner un avantage aux marques et aux clients qui font confiance à notre expérience. Marquer l'esprit des gens est notre objectif commun.

Name: Melissa Schatzmann - Realist Artist
Description:
Melissa Schatzmann is a professional Canadian realist artist. Her daily life is surrounded by artistic and creative projects. Melissa teaches children and adult drawing classes. Melissa has taught realistic pencil drawing classes through Loyalist College in Belleville, Ontario, Canada for the past 20 years. In 2003, she designed Canada’s first online pencil drawing classes offered through Ontario Community Colleges. Today, these online courses are being offered in over 13 Ontario Community Colleges which registers students from all across Canada.

Melissa’s pencil drawings have been published in a high school textbook through McGraw-Hill Ryerson as well as an international promotional brochure through Sakura Products of America for her earlier work in pointillism. She has showcased artwork in the Quinte region and has sold her work throughout Canada, U.S and Europe. Her coloured pencil work has been on the front cover of Ann Kullberg’s magazine, From My Perspective and recently she was featured in a CBC Art Series.

Melissa continues to be amazed by the wonderment of the world around her in creating true-life drawings of people, nature and animals in her work. She achieves high realism in each of her pencil drawings by paying close attention to detail in capturing the essence of her subjects. You can view Melissa’s Pencil Drawings in her online website gallery.

Melissa teaches children’s drawing classes in her home. If you think your child may be interested in these drawing classes please email her at contact@realisticdrawing.com

Name: WALL90
Description:
WALL90   ORIGINAL ART
WALL90 presents artist Paul Ygartua’s paintings for sale.
 Welcome to our curated collection of original art for sale! We are pleased to offer a wide range of styles and subjects to choose from, including abstracts, west coast scenes, and cubist artworks. Our abstract paintings are characterized by their bold, expressive style, which uses colour, shape, and form to convey emotion and meaning. Explore west coast scenes that capture the beauty and diversity of the west coast landscape, from the rugged coastline to the lush forests and mountains. We are confident that you will find something that speaks to your unique taste and style in our collection of original art.

These original paintings by local Vancouver artist Paul Ygartua can be shipped internationally and are available for purchase.

View Original Art For Sale ORIGINAL PAINTINGS FOR SALE  -- a new outlet for international contemporary artist Paul Ygartua where he can express his extraordinary talent on a larger scale buildings, offices, homes, restaurants and clubs interior and exterior. Subject matter can be varied, from Modern Abstracts, Masks, Musicians, Local and International scenes. Ygartua has worked on many large mural projects and is one of the few artists in the world who uses no projector or scale and paints everything freehand.

Name: Summer Weeds
Description:
Summer Weeds is a collection of nature-inspired artwork both modern and antique. Explore nature past and present by mixing eco-friendly and affordable modern handmade colorful plant silhouettes and vintage hundred-year-old prints. Antique prints by artists such as Pratt, Thornton and James Sowerby.

Our Mission: We work with integrity and ethics.
Your satisfaction with our products and service is important to us.  Our products are made with quality archival materials from sustainable, recycled, and cruelty-free sources when possible.

Explore
Delve deeper into the lives and legacies of the artists, writers, botanists, and intrepid explorers who played pivotal roles during the golden age of exploration. Discover the captivating stories behind these trailblazers who ventured into uncharted territories, chronicling their adventures through art, literature, and scientific discovery. Explore the intertwined worlds of creativity and curiosity as you uncover the remarkable contributions of these individuals to our understanding of the world and its wonders.

Discover
Explore an extensive glossary teeming with terms at the intersection of nature and art. Immerse yourself in a curated collection of words that illuminate the connections between the natural world and artistic expression.

Name: Ajabbi
Description:
I was born in 1956 and grew up in Invercargill in a happy family of 6. School holidays were always Stewart Island or going camping in the bush.

I always liked making things and so spent most of my time day dreaming. It was much more interesting than school, except for science subjects.

One day I found some clay and thought I might use it to figure out how metal bars deform when blacksmithing. I was holding this ball of clay in my hand - looked at it and thought - why don't I copy that - so 8 hours later - there was a clay hand holding a clay ball of clay.

27 years later - I'm still doing it because it's so much fun

Name: Ai Molti Tesori
Description:
Ai Molti Tesori è un luogo ampio e luminoso nel cuore di Mendrisio, pensato per accogliere e coltivare il potenziale creativo racchiuso in ognuno di noi. 

Si organizzano molteplici i laboratori esperienziali di arte individuali e di gruppo che vengono rivolte sia a adulti che bambini.

Promuove l'arteterapia nelle sue molteplici declinazioni: 
- 𝗟𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗶 𝗲 𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗰𝗼𝗿𝘀𝗶 𝗱𝗶 𝗔𝗿𝘁𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗽𝗶𝗮
Tramite i materiali artistici a disposizione e al processo creativo si entra in contatto con i propri vissuti emotivi profondi, dandogli forma, colore, volume sperimentando un qui e ora in sintonia con il vivere emozionale del momento. 
Esprimere la propria esperienza interna permette anche di riconoscerla, comprenderla, condividerla.  
Si attivano così risorse personali latenti, migliorando il proprio benessere psicofisico.

- 𝗟𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗿𝗼𝗿𝗶 𝗱𝗶 𝗙𝗼𝘁𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗽𝗶𝗮
Fotografia e arte terapeutica si fondono in un percorso nel quale l'immagine fotografica è l'elemento dal quale partire per strutture la relazione d'aiuto.

𝗔𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗼 𝗮 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗮𝘇𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗶 𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 a 360° accoglie idee e progetti dandogli spazio e implementando sinergie.
- Organizza, accoglie e promuove 𝗰𝗼𝗿𝘀𝗶, 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀𝗵𝗼𝗽, 𝘀𝗲𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗿𝗶, 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘃𝗲𝗴𝗻𝗶, 𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝘀, 𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶, 𝗲𝘀𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗶, 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘇𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗶 𝗱𝗶 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶 𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹𝗶 e sociali finalizzati al dialogo, alla conoscenza e allo sviluppo di risorse e del benessere personali e collettivi
- Offre uno 𝘀𝗽𝗮𝘇𝗶𝗼 𝗰𝗼𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗼
- Offre uno 𝘀𝗽𝗮𝘇𝗶𝗼 𝗲𝘀𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗼 - 𝗴𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗮, 𝗹𝘂𝗼𝗴𝗼 𝗱'𝗶𝗻𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼 𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘇𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗲 
- Si occupa del 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗿𝗰𝗶𝗼 𝗱𝗶 𝗼𝗴𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶 𝗱'𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲, artigianato, interior design, complementi d'arredo, libri, oggettistica etnica, accessori per la meditazione ed il benessere. 

Per info info@aimoltitesori.ch

Description: SBFA is a team of art consultants operating out of two fine art galleries: San Francisco and Menlo Park. We specialize in contemporary paintings, sculpture and photography and our artists range from emerging to mid-career. Our services include complimentary in-home and in-office curating, framing, commissioning custom artwork, and complimentary delivery & installation. Contact us at info@sbfinearts.com for one of our art consultants to visit your home or office or to schedule a tour of our San Francisco or Menlo Park gallery.

Name: ArtMindGallery
Description:
ArtMindGallery præsenterer en udstillingsplatform, hvor kunstnere kan udtrykke sig, som de lyster. Vores fokus er rettet mod kunstnere, der betragter verden i gadehøjde.

Portrætter og visuelle fortolkninger af gadens grimsmukke miljø, de grå betonvægge, nattens puls
og røgfyldte værtshuse fylder vores vægge ud. 
  
Vi udstiller kun kunstnere, som vi kan mærke, og som vi føler vitterligt har noget på hjerte.
  
Hos ArtMindGallery anerkender vi kunstnere for det de kan, og ikke hvad de kan indtjene.
  
Vi elsker, når kunstnere søger nye udtryk, og vi nyder at bidrage hertil ved at give dem frie tøjler i vores udstillingslokaler i midten af smilets by.
  
I vores udstillingslokaler bruger kunstneren hele rummet til at udstille og udtrykke sig. Det giver besøgende oplevelser, hvor de kan lure med, når det legende og den gennemførte udførelse mødes i favntag, der huskes.
  
Derudover sætter vi også en stor ære i at præsentere kunstneren bag de udstillede værker. Vi giver publikum adgang til viden om kunstneren som person. Vi håber derved at kunne give indblik i de bevæggrunde, universer og verdensopfattelser, der ligger bag de udstillede værker.
  
Hos ArtMindGallery er der nye udstillinger hver 2. måned med danske såvel som udenlandske kunstnere.
  
ArtMind Gallery har til huse på Frederiksgade 12, 1. Sal, 8000 Aarhus C.

ArtMind Gallery har åben torsdag -  fredag kl. 16.00 - 18.00 og lørdag kl. 11:00 - 13:00. 

Udenfor åbningstid kontakt Gallerist Michael Lund på Tlf:. 53 83 55 58

Name: BEK – Bergen Centre for Electronic Arts
Description:
BEK – Bergen Centre for Electronic Arts – is an interdisciplinary production centre for art and new technology. BEK develops, produces and communicates projects in the intersection of contemporary art, technology, theory and social critique.

BEK arranges workshops and courses for artists in different stages of their careers, and offers advising and development support to artists and art organisations. Through the residency program BEK opens its studios and production facilities to over 40 local and international artists annually. BEK´s discursive programme explores theoretical and ethical aspects of the technological development.

At BEK, you will find a supportive and encouraging community of artists and collaborators. We work locally, nationally and internationally on collaborative projects and knowledge exchange. The organisations and venues in our network are committed to sharing experimental art and music with a wide audience.

Name: Love And Lobby
Description:
*a subjective, yet consistent cartography of contemporary talent, from countries less known in the West, countries with an unfair stigma attached in the mainstream media, other times just associated with clichés that are no longer relevant or totally insignificant for the bigger picture.

*when the evening news around the world starts, the focus is on politics, politicians and usually bad vibes. When there is none of the above, there is silence. Is there another way to look at the identity of a country, nowadays and to discover it, in a completely different key? Through this platform we hope to give the answer.

*carefully curated content for international, badass headhunters in search of fresh, educated, sparkling creatives, from a wide range of disciplines, with proven results.

*a lobby tool for a country’s reputation abroad, via some of its remarkable citizens & their contribution to the world.

*a microfiber cloth to wipe clean the clichés of a country and its people.

*an alternative to Lonely Planet for people willing to explore countries of the world coolest places through the lens of the recommended designers.

*a light, subtle way to bring harmony & equal amount of attention to both talented men and women.

What is not:
*a movement that wants to ignore, downgrade or extinct men.

*an attempt to resurrect the women warriors’ empire.

How it works:
THE GENTLEMAN

An influential professional gentleman recommends an outstanding lady in the creative industry. (award-winner, talented, bright & able to handle international projects).
*For the some countries, we invited also women to recommend female talent.

THE LADY

She has her portrait taken in a location of her choosing, ideally belonging to the designer she recommends. She tells us about herself and gives a mini-interview (see questions below).

THE DESIGNER

A culturally and national identity relevant designer’s work is presented, as endorsed by the lady.

FSRD ITB 2024 is a faculty based student organization, managed by over 400 undergraduate Faculty of Arts and Design scholars of 2024 at Bandung Institute of Technology. FSRD ITB 2024 has managed a total of 8 events in the first year including SINARUPA, an academic art exhibition that has welcomed over 6,000 visitors and collaborated with 17 sponsors.

Name: Indigo Blue Art
Description:
INDIGO BLUE ART was established in Singapore in 2004 as a platform to showcase Contemporary Indian Art. 

Committed to promoting a greater awareness, understanding, and enjoyment of Indian Art, INDIGO BLUE ART houses an extensive collection of paintings, drawings, prints, sculptures and books on the subject.

Our vision is to curate exhibitions which highlight the breadth and diversity of Indian art. Our collection covers various forms from naïve folk and tribal art, high calibre works by the fabled masters and senior artists, to the talented work emerging in India today. We aim to provide a historical context whilst reflecting on important trends in Contemporary Indian Art. 

Our passion is creating a wider audience for Indian Art and to help further a better understanding of the subject. We offer a series of mixed and solo exhibitions throughout the year which provide a unique insight into the artists’ world, serving to stimulate an exchange of ideas and allow for engagement with the work on many different levels. 

Appreciation and understanding of art comes from experiencing and feeling. We look forward to welcoming art lovers of all levels to INDIGO BLUE ART.

Name: Portland Immersive Media Group
Description:
Precipice Fund 2015 Grant Recipient

The awe-striking power of virtual reality is evident to most anyone who tries it: a visceral feeling of being immersed in a fantasy, a full body epiphany one must experience first hand to believe. It’s as thrilling as it is “uncanny” or potentially disquieting.

For the members of PIMG (a collective of digital and new media artists), these new immersive technologies represent new possibilities igniting our imagination. We see them as precipitating a paradigm shift toward a new era of multi-sensory communication, one with uniquely empathic, beatific, and therapeutic potential. 

Offsetting this promise however are the high costs of entry: namely, the expense of purchasing the necessary equipment and sufficient computer hardware to power the experiences. 

Because these costs are prohibitive, especially to artists for whom commercial gains may not be a priority, help is needed to enable the development of experimental works which can engender critical dialogue and offer counterpoint to the Silicon Valley-fueled growth paradigm, or the established methods of the video game industry.

Our interest is in the development of experimental VR and mixed reality experiences to be presented to the public at alternating venues/locales as a means of conducting pioneering inquiries into this emergent medium.

Name: THE FRENCH GIRL STUDIO
Description:
Tronel's art embodies the power of creativity and individual action in reshaping our relationship with the world. The "Giant Crushed Cans" series invites viewers to embrace sustainability and sparks conversations on environmental consciousness.

Her work has graced the likes of K11 MUSEA, the Hong Kong Convention Centre, the French Consul-General’s residence, Digital Art Fair Asia, Soho House and the French May Arts Festival, while being featured in a variety of international publications.

A documentary featuring the series is also in production, set to be showcased at the International Movie Festival on Sustainable Development in Hong Kong in May 2024.

Besides her Crushed Cans work - a statement on consumer culture, recycling and reimagining the disposable - Tronel’s twist on the Chinese symbol of Double Happiness - the Shining Heart - can be seen around town, symbolising souls intertwined and the need for (re)connection.

Welcome, be inspired!

Union Street Gallery’s mission is to facilitate a variety of engaging arts experiences and opportunities that support the creative growth of artists and art appreciators alike, while also contributing to the cultural landscape of Chicago’s Southland.

Union Street Gallery features two floors of gallery space. We strive to present a variety of work from regional and national juried, curated, and invited exhibits. Additionally, we highlight are from the local community in our classroom throughout the year. 15 artists are members of Union Street Gallery and maintain studio space throughout the building. Name: LetsMakeStoriesDino
Description:
LetsMakeStoriesDino is an online writing community to roar through words, stories and emotions.

LetsMakeStoriesDino's aim is in bringing all the people into writing,
Because we speak at least 5000-40000 words per day and, why not put 5-10% of those spilled every day into a writing piece.
 
Why not put those emotions on a page?
Nothing will change around us, even we may not at times.
At least something caged through words and stories travels out and finds solace in a document and creates an opportunity to be read and heard.
There by creates an ecosystem.
 
Should our writing always fly?
- It need not always fly like an eagle
- Our writing can be like that tortoise which can swim in water and snail on the ground.
- Our writing can be like that termite which builds hill for the others
- Our writing can be like that penguin which enjoys its stays in the Antarctic finding solace in one particular habitat
And our writing can be like that Banquo who can beget Kings, we meant yet another Writer.

And if you wish to be part of writing events or books, write to us on
letsmakestoriesdino@gmail.com

Through LetsMakeStoriesDino in association with ArtoonsInn Geeks Room, The Archaic House and Pachyderm Tales, we have published following books and they are available on Amazon and other book buying marketplace.

1) Arising from the Dust, journeyed by 32 writers, a project by LetsMakeStoriresDino 
Ranked as No.1 in Amazon Bestseller and Hot new releases 
https://rb.gy/hit8de 

2) Anklets in My Hands, a Poetry connect by 32 Women Poets..
https://rb.gy/1t1wcu 

Our next project is Fire spoke to Water, a poetry connect due for release in April 2022

Name: Anders Wahlstedt Fine Art
Description:

Description: Established in London in 2022, OMNI represents a diverse roster of multidisciplinary modern and contemporary artists. DOMIN Poznań jest oparty na bazie ponad 20 letniego doświadczenia w nauczaniu rysunku i malarstwa. Nasi absolwenci są obecnie studentami na uczelniach architektonicznych i artystycznych w całej Polsce, a wielu z nich obecnie jest doświadczonymi projektantami, artystami, architektami czy wykładowcami.

Te sukcesy to nie przypadek, pracownia DOMIN jest zbudowania na pasji, ambicji i energii założycieli i prowadzących zajęciach.

Te cechy wzbogacone indywidulanym podejściem i doskonałą atmosferą zajęć sprawią, że każdy kto chce realizować swoje rysunkowe pasje znajdzie u Nas doskonałe miejsce do rozwoju.

Sprawdź co mówią o Nas nasi absolwenci, a najlepiej przekonaj się sam i dołącz do Nas na zajęciach!

Name: The Humble Hunter
Description:
I create Intuitive Art, which is artwork that is able to have a conversation with your subconscious mind. You will have the ability to understand the message without the need for any conscious reasoning. Giving a simple but powerful message without any effort required on your behalf.

I create my Intuitive Art using nature, colour psychology, and the ancient Japanese art form of Kintsugi to inspire an emotional and physiological response, and also to reflect life’s challenges to allow people to feel acknowledged, supported and recognise their achievement in moving forward through adversity.

Let us help you showcase your art in its best light. Visit our showroom or contact us today for a personalized consultation.

sarahbalinskas.com

Name: Ondrash & Kašpárek
Description:
Artistic duo Ondřej Konupčík (1984) and Radim Kašpárek (1990) creating through a technique they have developed based on a painting on the water.

They have met each other when Ondrash tattooed Radim and they both found a similar passion in painting. For more than 6 years they went through a development of a very unique technique of organic painting.

“We considered painting to be a good way of emotional catharsis and soul purification for a long time, and only later did we realise that we had a much deeper relationship with it. We have rediscovered the joy of pure creation, where, regardless of the result and evaluation, we try everything the technique and medium allow us. The result is a very distinctive way of painting, which amazes us to this day every time we do it thanks to its diversity and at the same time it inspires us for further work.”

Name: Grünewald Guild
Description:
The Grünewald Guild welcomes and inspires all who seek to explore the relationships between art, faith & community.

The Grunewald Guild welcomes and affirms all humans. Each person – every age, size, color, gender identity, sexual orientation, family structure, race, religion, country of origin, ability level and language – is important and necessary to us. We embrace and care for each entrant into our space, valuing them as part of our fellowship. We believe that diversity is a beautiful way to build a community.

The Grünewald Guild believes in the transformational power of the arts.  In support of this value, we provide a nurturing environment for creative discovery and artistic expression.

Rooted in the ecumenical Christian tradition, the Grünewald Guild welcomes seekers of all faith backgrounds, and respects the diverse spiritual paths that often lead to our open door.

Life in community is at the heart of the Grünewald Guild – a foundation for inspiration.  Guests, volunteers, and staff alike engage in shared experiences of creative expression, rest, work, play and reflection.

At the Grünewald Guild, our staff and volunteers take pride in offering genuine hospitality; ensuring our guests are cared for in a friendly manner, nourished by healthy food, in a clean, comfortable, welcoming environment.

The Grünewald Guild practices sustainability.  This value is embodied through attention to caring for the earth and her gifts, and through sound financial practices and thoughtful stewardship of the gifts of time, talent and financial resources that are offered by a vast community of love and support.

Name: Start Up and Dance
Description:
It is the mission and duty of Start Up and Dance to see to it that our clients are the most successful organizations and artists in their segment of the dance industry. We will lead the art of dance into its greatest era of prosperity by utilizing effective business practices that maximize efficiency, and developing the most innovative marketing strategies and promotional campaigns the art form has ever known.

The art of dance is the greatest form of non-verbal communication in the world, and the Dance Community is full of extremely talented artist who would much rather use their time and efforts to focus on their art, than on a ticket sales strategy, organizational budgets, or business to business campaigns. Whether you own a dance studio or competition, direct a dance company, convention, or festival, or are a freelance teacher, choreographer, or artist, Start Up and Dance can handle all of the business aspects of your dance entity, bolstering you and your organization to even greater levels of success.

Name: Olga Gouskova
Description:
Often walking hand-in-hand with controversy is the sensual female form, Russian born, Belgium based - artist Olga Gouskova certainly knows how to capture an audience with her paintings of beautiful and stylish women. Her paintings are about feelings and states of mood, about dreams and expectations. And she express it through the female portrait. In every painting she tries to capture part of the mystery of the female personality by combining the beauty of the physical with the intensity of the mental.
Olga Gouskova was born 28 July 1974 in Moscow, Russia. She grew up in Belarus.
1996 immigrated to Belgium. Since 2002 she lives, loves and works in Bruges.

cell 519 709 1030.  Email:
darleanmorris@sympatico.ca

Name: Tottering by Gently
Description:
Annie Tempest is one of Britain’s best-loved cartoonists.  For her popular strip cartoon ‘The Yuppies’ which ran for 7 years in the Daily Mail, she was recognised by her peers in the Cartoonists Club of Great Britain as ‘Strip Cartoonist of the year’.  In 1993, Annie embarked on her current internationally acclaimed cartoon strip ‘Tottering-by-Gently’ for Country Life magazine.  

In 1995, The O’Shea Gallery, St. James’s Street, London was appointed agent for Annie Tempest and Tottering-by-Gently originals and publisher of her books and prints.  In 2009, The Cartoon Art Trust awarded her the prestigious Pont Prize for ‘The Portrayal of The British Character’. Her now international following proves that she touches a note of universal truth in her beautifully executed detailed cartoons as she laughs with us at the stuff of life.

Tottering-by-Gently is now a well-known brand and a visit to the website www.tottering.com is recommended, as is a visit to The O’Shea Gallery at No. 4 St.James’s Street, London SW1A 1EF where the gallery displays all of Annie’s original watercolours available plus her signed numbered edition prints as well as her books and the full range of Tottering product.

Name: Rebecca Tunks Artist
Description:
Rebecca Lee is an upcoming, passionate young photographer whose works bring out the deepest messages with the simplest compositions.

Her education in Arts stems back to a Special Arts Acceptance program in High School, where extended art classes taught her a variety of artistic mediums, through to a Advanced Diploma in Photography and most recently the completion of a Bachelor of Communications & Contemporary Arts degree at ECU (Major Photography).

It was during her university education era combined with her travels that she decided to progress away from commercial photography and only photograph artistic representations.

Her most recent series of works are inspired by her 2 years living in India and the process of cultural adaption to a foreign environment during this time. She works by selecting and photographing small inanimate objects that are common to most cultures and repetitively placing them in collages, which creates a visual exploration of these objects. Their unity from afar and their absolute variety up close, is the interactive play of travel that she is endeavoring to represent.

This passion for & style of photography has thus been continued through Switzerland, Australia, London, Hong Kong, Macau and Austria.

Name: Studio Michael Müller
Description:
Michael Anthony Müller is a Berlin-based conceptual artist whose artworks have been featured in world-renowned museums and in some of the most important commercial galleries across Europe, Asia, and the United States. For nearly 20 years, Michael has pushed the creative boundaries in all types of experiential and performance art through paintings, drawings, text-based work, found objects, sculptures, and music. Michael is known for creating art as a language that transcends the psychological and emotional constructs of the human form in ways that challenge conventional thinking, introduce diverse psychological worlds, and inspire self-exploration.

He was born in Ingelheim am Rhein, Germany into a German/British-Indian family, and spent many years in Ladakh, Kashmir. It was there that he began his professional career and developed a very specialized drawing technique, and progressed as a painter. He also studied sculpture at Kunstakademie Dusseldorf with Magdalena Jetelová.

Michael has showcased his wide range of artworks in solo and group exhibitions at major institutions within Germany and internationally, including the KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Staatliche Kunsthalle Baden-Baden, Kunsthalle Bremen, Städtische Galerie Wolfsburg, Bundeskunsthalle Bonn, and Brandenburgischer Kunstverein Potsdam. Additionally, he frequently collaborates with other emerging and established artists on projects that culminate in experience-rich installations and exhibitions. His work is recognized as being reflective of a diverse self, in distinct moments of time within an ever-changing world.

Name: TS+ Projects
Description:
Tali Wertheimer and Simmy Swinder launched TS+ Projects in April 2010 with a pop-up exhibition "Happiness Is" in Manhattan's West Village. As an art production and curating team, TS+ Projects has worked with PULSE, Gawker, Benrimon Contemporary, Ligne Roset, The Lucie Foundation, and Young Projects at the Pacific Design Center. Most recently Simmy and Tali took on the roles of Fair Manager and Director of Programming for photo l.a. XX | artLA projects 2011. Tali has worked at Sotheby's, Metro Pictures, and Paul Kasmin Gallery. Simmy worked at Santa Maria Della Scala in Siena, Italy, Los Angeles Municipal Art Gallery, and Cottelston Advisors. Their projects have been
reviewed by Modern Painters, Artnet, The Huffington Post, The LA Times, and the Village Voice. Tali received her BA in Art History with a minor in Economics, NYU. Simmy received her BA in Philosophy with a minor in Art History, UC Berkeley and her Master's in Art Business, Sotheby's Institute of Art New York. Visit www.tsplusprojects.com to learn more.

Name: Tilki Sanat I Atölye
Description:
Kültür ve sanat alanında disiplinlerarası ilişkiler kurarak üretim ve eleştiri süreçlerine odaklanır.
Biçimsel ve içeriksel kategorilerden oluşan bir sanat yazımı yerine, üretim süreçlerini tarihsel ve bireysel bağlamda yaşanan zamanın izdüşümleri olarak değerlendirir.
Endüstriyelleşmiş galeri anlayışı yerine sanatçı ve sanatseverlerin günlük yaşam rutini içinde süregelen bir sergileme deneyimi yaşatmayı hedefler. Yapıtın cansız bir teşhir nesnesi olarak değil, anlık deneyimlenen ortamın bir paydaşı olarak algılanmasına yönelir.
Eleştiri aracılığıyla yapıtın içinde bulunduğu kültürü parçalara ayırarak, bir yapıtı oluşturan bütün unsurların kültür içerisindeki işleyiş biçimlerini ortaya çıkarmayı amaçlar.
Kültür ve sanat yaşamına alternatif bir sosyal alan sunar.
Farklı disiplinlerdeki sanatçıların birlikte çalışması için uygun alanlar yaratır.
Sanatçı, sanatsever ve sanat öğrencilerine yönelik ortak kullanıma açık bir dökümantasyon havuzu oluşturur, enformasyon sağlar ve kültür-sanat alanında yayıncılık faaliyeti yürütür.
Üretici ve alımlayıcı arasında dolaysız bir iletişimin sürekliliğini hedefler. Sanatçı ve sanatsever arasındaki “Sanat” diye büyük harfle yazılan sınırı kaldırmayı amaçlar.
Kültür sanat sahasında kent hafızasını önemser, yapacağı çalışmalarda bu birikimi başat bir değer olarak görür. Bu bağlamda yerel kurum ve kuruluşlar ile ortak çalışmalarda bulunur.
Kentin ortak hafızası ile sanat arasında bağ kurmaya odaklanır.
Sanatın günlük hayatta daha etkin bir rol oynaması için farklı mesleki grupların yaklaşım ve bakış açılarından yararlanmayı önceler.

Name: The Cobbs Auctioneers
Description:
The Cobbs Auctioneers is a professional family-owned and operated auction house located in the historic Mill building known as Noone Falls Mill, in the quaint New England town of Peterborough NH. One of our primary missions is to provide a transparent and enjoyable auction experience for both buyers and sellers. We take extra measures to both expose our consignors offerings to an international market and give our buyer customers the confidence to participate and make purchases, in an auction setting. We share a love of art, culture and history with our customers and know that honesty and disclosure are crucial elements for successful transactions at auction. We strive to instill and promote a sense of stewardship and passion for art, antiques and cultural objects. Like many of our customers we share a belief that objects have meaning and value above and beyond their investment, but we do not take that investment for granted.

We specialize in 19th century or earlier Americana and Fine Art though our auctions cover a wide range of approximately 50 major collecting categories.  We are fully licensed and bonded as an Auctioneer in the state of New Hampshire and on a cooperative basis in other states. One of the benefits of operating in NH is that the state does not have a sales tax which translates to more money for our consigners and less expense for our buyers.  We are partnered with Artfact/Invaluable, Auctionzip and LiveAuctioneers for the convenience of our customers who prefer to bid online.

Name: ACTE SEPT OFFICIEL
Description:
Le domaine de la culture au Mali a connu un tournant décisif suite à l’avènement de la démocratie en 1991 par une foulée de création de mouvements associatifs.
Acte SEPT est né à Bamako en 1994.

Emboîtant le pas de la liberté d’expression, l’association a pour but de contribuer à la promotion de la culture malienne et de favoriser l’épanouissement de ses membres à travers des activités artistiques et culturelles

Le nom de l’association possède une origine et une signification singulières.
Le terme « acte » témoigne de la persévérance, de la vivacité et de la diversité des actions posées par l’organisation.
Le choix du chiffre sept réfère aux chiffres trois et quatre. Le premier est réservé aux hommes dans la philosophie malienne tandis que le second se rapporte aux femmes. L’homme et la femme sont donc réunis pour lutter pour la cause de la culture.

Name: Chautauqua Visual Arts
Description:
Centered on four pillars—School of Art, Galleries, Lecture Series & Events and Community Classes—this is a space to experience all that our Chautauqua Visual Arts community has to offer.

The Chautauqua School of Art residency program is a centerpiece of the Chautauqua Visual Arts (CVA). Founded in 1909, the School is an incubator of inclusive and expansive programming in the visual arts composed of workshops and one-on-one time with mentors covering all media. The curriculum intentionally breaks apart the traditional methods that silo disciplines from each other and instead embraces a full range of studio and pragmatic studies including professional development for artists, the art of pedagogy, writing in the 21st century, archiving and the relevance of art history today. Our program is inclusive, which is reflected not only by our team who teach at Chautauqua Visual Arts but also conveyed by the knowledge they share.

Name: SPYRAL documentary
Description:
Bipolar Documentary, LLC, a Limited Liability Company, is an independent motion picture production company assembled from creative and business entertainment industry professionals with a common goal of producing the independent film (working title) "Bipolar". 

The Project 
"Bipolar" is a feature documentary budgeted to be produced at $498,000, intended for both theatrical exhibition and digital distribution. The film genre is documentary, with attributes of mental health, bipolar, suicide, and addiction. With the producers and director having direct relationships with star talent and representation by William Morris Agency, Gersh and United Talent Artists Agency, the Company desires to hire name talent to narrate the film, thereby adding to the film's commercial value and audience appeal. Over 90% of the film is comprised of archival materials that can be edited remotely. The company is setup for virtual reviews that will expedite this project during COVID-19 shutdowns putting it ahead of films that have had to postpone their productions. 

Current Marketplace during COVID 
The COVID pandemic has turned our world upside down. In the film industry this has had very negative and positive impacts simultaneously. While productions have halted and theaters stopped premiering films, the streaming industry has exploded. With the dramatic increase in demand due to millions of streamers being confined to their homes, the streaming platforms are desperate for more content. This has "Bipolar" poised to be an extremely timely film. Not only is mental health at the forefront of policy, healthcare and homes around the world, but finishing the movie is very COVID friendly. Comprising largely home videos, we only require a few days with a small production crew to complete principal photography. 

Collectively, the production team of "Bipolar" has produced a variety of television programs, including hundreds of hours of network and cable television.

Name: Tacoma City Ballet
Description: In 1955, Miss Jan Collum founded The Jan Collum School of Classical Ballet and The Concert Ballet Group of Tacoma. The School to impart excellent classical ballet instruction, and the Company to perform classical ballets with a stellar standard of excellence and professionalism. The Concert Ballet Group of Tacoma began with six Company Dancers who soon blossomed into thirty. By 1965, The Concert Ballet Group of Tacoma was renamed BALLETACOMA and established as a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Corporation. BALLETACOMA, under the Artistic Direction of Miss Jan Collum, forged a local, regional, and national reputation for excellence in classical ballet instruction and performance for the next thirty years. In 1995, BALLETACOMA was re-christened Tacoma City Ballet which combined both the School and the Company under a single 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Corporation. Since 1955, The Concert Ballet Group of Tacoma, BALLETACOMA, and now Tacoma City Ballet have presented an annual performance season in Tacoma, as well as touring productions to other regional cities. With the opening of the Pantages Theater in 1983, Miss Jan Collum realized her ultimate dream and was able to establish a tradition of an annual performance season in a professional theater for her Company and a live professional orchestra. This tradition has continued from 1983 to the present. Tacoma City Ballet has resided in the beautiful Merlino Art Center since 1983, and recently created The Jan Collum Ballroom Theater, a fully equipped black box theater in the Merlino Art Center, to host community performances. In this majestic historical building that is one of the jewels in Tacoma’s architectural crown, Tacoma City Ballet studies, rehearses, and performs the art of classical ballet.

Name: Galleries at Lynn Arts
Description: The Galleries At LynnArts (GALA) is an arts based collaborative that is in the process of becoming a Collaborative Nonprofit Art Gallery.  It is lead by a small group of local artists and art organizers who have designed two galleries and a gallery shop.  These spaces are used to exhibit group and solo shows as well as house a handmade shop filled with arts and crafts produced by North Shore artists and makers.  We work with artists and makers at every stages of their career.  Emerging artists are given support in developing their work, preparing work for professional presentation and exhibition, and access to the local gallery system.  GALA is a community where the gallery spaces and outside events are used to promote and sell artwork from those artists who traditionally have had access to galleries and to invite makers who might not have had access into the space and honor their products and objects equally.  We create community through exhibit receptions, artists' sales and fairs, and by using the gallery spaces for public and private events.  It is our intention to support the whole artist by creating programming to promote skills growth, wellbeing, and foster a healthy art community.

Name: Unending Emanation

Taking place from the 7th to the 9th of October, 2021, we invite audiences to step into this glowing era that we have recreated through paintings, sculptures, and installations. Focusing on the themes of movement, symbolism, and femininity, we have collaborated with artists from diverse backgrounds to demonstrate how the City of Light has stood the test of time with help from la Belle Époque.

Name: Andrea Marchetti Art
Description:
ANDREA MARCHETTI
Nato in Umbria nel 1990 a Castiglione del Lago.
In costante contatto con le realtà socialmente impegnate della zona, sviluppa una crescente sensibilità per quelle tematiche che diventeranno fondamentali nel suo lavoro: denuncia, lotta, tradizione e condivisione.

La vicinanza al movimento del Graffitismo lo spinge ad approfondire le potenzialità narrative della pittura nel contesto urbano.

Plasma la sua arte vivendo in alcune delle capitali più vivaci d’Europa. Roma, Madrid, Lisbona e Berlino sono le città che hanno maggiormente influenzato il suo stile, caratterizzato dall’uso di colori accesi e vibranti.

Al contempo, la passione per la fotografia e l’esperienza come Grafico e Visual Artist hanno ampliato il suo sguardo e le sue capacità tecniche. Nelle sue opere è apprezzabile una continua commistione tra analogico e digitale, un dialogo serrato tra passato e futuro.

Name: Write On, Light On
Description:
Write On, Light On is a newly formed community interest company, set up by Jude Simmons and Charlotte Delaney.  We deliver creative writing workshops and courses designed to facilitate and encourage personal development, communication, imagination, literacy skills, self confidence and the skills to structure a story or a script. We are experienced in producing and managing large and small community projects, writing for radio, stage and screen and have worked closely with academics, including at Oxford University. Charlotte Delaney was personal assistant to her mother Shelagh Delaney, (A Taste of Honey, Dance With a Stranger et al) for over 15 years, helping with story and script development, script editing, proof reading and giving robust, fearless notes.
Our workshops are for everybody: as a non profit organisation we are committed to bringing them to marginalised and vulnerable groups, but they are equally effective - and enjoyable - as team building exercises in the business world or for those who would like to explore an idea for a story or script - but don't know where to start.
Creative writing, especially with pen and paper, helps us communicate complex ideas more effectively, can broaden our vocabulary, focus and calm our minds....the list is endless. We don't have to want to be best-sellers to exercise our creative minds. Just like we don't have to be competing in the olympics to enjoy physical exercise. It's just good for us, and that's good enough. 
If you are interested in finding out more about what Write On, Light On offers, please contact us at: writeonlighton@proton.me or via dm on LinkedIn whilst our website is under construction.
We look forward to hearing from you.

Customers can discuss framing options and have no obligation quotes. Why not opt to have your art collection framed, or reframed, in matching frames.

Name: Boston Mural
Description:
Boston Mural specializes in breathing life into office spaces and transforming them into beautiful works of art. We serve the very best in Boston Tech and beyond.

At Boston Mural, our niche is at the intersection of Art and Industry. We believe in replacing those dull grey walls and cubicles of yesteryear with vibrant colors, textures + branding that enhances the employee experience. We strive to make your office a place where your team loves to work, that wows your clients when they visit. To sum it up: being at work should no longer feel like just a job, but a lifestyle, and we make the kind of environment that supports that lifestyle. 

Helping you, our customer, begins in the consultation phase. We do our homework before meeting you in person, and come to the table with a design board, inspired by your company brand and mission. We then photograph your space and superpose digital mock-ups of several themes based on ideas from our discussions. Once a design is decided upon we work efficiently and courteously in your space to realize your vision. We keep you updated regularly and make the process feel relaxed and easy every step of the way. 

Contact us at BostonMural.com to schedule your consultation. We can't wait to hear from you!

TESTIMONIALS: 
"Our HqO mural is really the key to our office. It has meaning with regards to our customers and our culture and Boston Mural did a phenomenal job working with our team to determine the right design for our office. She also works quite quickly while maintaining a really high standard of quality. We will most certainly be using her again in the future!"   - Chase Garbarino, HqO CEO

"Boston Mural helped transform DailyBreak's headquarters from a plain office to a vibrant work of art by collaborating with our interior designer to coordinate colors. ...We can't recommend them enough" - Jared Stenquist, DailyBreak Co-founder + former CTO.

Description:

IUA is an independent, coeducational, business and liberal arts international institution of higher learning. As a foreign university in London, IUA is independently administered in accordance to the education reform Act 1988 by Management Education Resources Centre (MERC) incorporated (as a college of higher education) in England and Wales in July 1995.


The university provides an adequate and satisfactory international standard of education and confers diplomas and degree mainly in the business area. The university is a member of European Council for Business Education (ECBE). It is also an approved and accredited centre for the Council for Licensed Conveyancers (UK). IUA, until recently, was a UK registered Education Provider under the auspices and authority of DfES (also known as the Department for Innovation, Universities and Skills) and conformed to the evolving rules, regulations and policies of the Home Office in the UK.

Name: Women in Art Organisation of Trinidad and Tobago
Description:
The Women in Art Organisation of Trinidad and Tobago (WIAOTT) is a not-for-profit group of professional women artists that advocate and support our members and associate members through art exhibitions, courses and a simple sense of community, mutual respect, and togetherness. Our list of diverse members represents all genres of the visual arts from across Trinidad and Tobago.

As an organisation, we work with both primary and secondary school students to foster the pursuit of careers within the art industry.

ABOUT THE WIAOTT: 
On the 20th July, 1996, Mrs. Fraulein Rudder formed The Women In Art Organisation of Trinidad and Tobago with just twenty members.

Among these pioneers are Former First Lady, Mrs Zalayhar Hassanali, Hetty De Gannes, Valerie Belgrave, Karen Sylvester, Lisa O’Connor, Maryse Rostant, Gillian Bishop and Sarah Beckett.

The organisation now boasts an increasing number of over one hundred and twenty members, comprised of art teachers, professionals, amateurs and aspiring artists in Trinidad and Tobago, with women artists from other Caribbean islands indicating their desire to join and participate in the Women in Art Organisation’s grand, annual exhibition.

As another initiative Mrs Rudder, once a teacher, partnered with the Ministry of Education to introduce The WIAOTT Biennial Art Competition and Exhibition among Fifth and Sixth Form Students in Trinidad and Tobago. This has wide participation from many schools which produces notably creative and excellent work. To this day, the WIAOTT has continued to be pioneers in the local art industry.

Name: Ars Pulchra
Description:
Ars Pulchra è una società fondata in Francia nel 2020.
I componenti di questo gruppo visionario provengono da mondi diversi: innovazione tecnologica, imprenditoria, comunicazione e arte.

Un incontro che nasce da un legame famigliare e si sviluppa attorno alla condivisione di un'idea: il mondo ha bisogno di bellezza.

Ars Pulchra si pone l'obiettivo di riscoprire la bellezza, riportandola al centro della società. Un progetto che nasce dalla condivisione dell'idea che il mondo abbia bisogno di bellezza e che vuole promuovere quel desiderio di bellezza, insito in ogni uomo, in grado di unire la conoscenza del cuore e la ricerca del vero. Un desiderio che non si limita a concepire cose e forme semplicemente da usare, ma che coltiva l'atteggiamento della riflessione e dello stupore nei confronti della realtà.

Ars Pulchra si rivolge dunque a tutte quelle organizzazioni, quegli individui e quelle istituzioni che credono profondamente che solo attraverso la bellezza, intesa nel suo senso più profondo ed estensivo, sia possibile agire per portare nel mondo in cui viviamo un reale miglioramento.

The studio is located on the third floor of Lowe Mill Arts & Entertainment in Huntsville, Alabama.  Come see the artist at work during public hours: Wed & Thurs 12 - 6pm, Fri 12 - 8 pm, Sat 12 - 6pm, or by appointment.  For examples of his work, please check out http://www.jameslambert.net.    

Lambert is a native of Huntsville, Alabama with a BFA in painting from the Rhode Island School of Design.

Name: Verein Sofalesungen
Description:
Sofalesungen.ch bringt Literatur nach Hause: Die öffentlichen Lesungen in privaten Räumen sind persönlich, originell und unkonventionell. Sie finden in WG-Wohnzimmern, Ateliers, Hinterhöfen oder Schrebergärten statt und bringen lesende und schreibende Menschen zusammen. Das kuratierte Programm präsentiert Autorinnen und Autoren aus der Schweiz und anderswo mit einem speziellen Fokus auf überzeugende Prosa-Debüts. Die Gastgeberinnen und Gastgeber sind Leute wie du und ich, leidenschaftliche oder verhinderte LeserInnen, Professorinnen und Müllmänner. Durch den direkten Kontakt zwischen AutorInnen und Publikum entsteht eine einzigartige Atmosphäre und jede Lesung ist ein ganz besonderes Erlebnis.

Zu einer Lesung gehört ein Text, ein Autor oder eine Autorin und natürlich das Publikum, daran halten wir fest. Doch die Möglichkeiten innerhalb dieses Rahmens sind vielfältig. Im persönlichen Rahmen einer Sofalesung lässt es sich optimal mit dem Format Lesung experimentieren, dessen Möglichkeiten und Grenzen spielerisch bis poetisch ausloten: Mal wird eine Moderation gewürfelt, mal kommen Musikinstrumente dazu, mal verschwimmen die Genregrenzen von Lyrik und Prosa. Das Ziel ist immer, Literatur lebendig zu vermitteln und Leselust zu schüren. Die Experimente, die funktionieren, behalten wir nicht für uns, sondern geben sie frei. Sie sollen – ganz open source – allen Interessierten zum Nachahmen und Weiterentwickeln zur Verfügung stehen.

Die Idee der Sofalesungen hat ihren Ursprung im Format «Zwischen/Miete» des Literaturbüros Freiburg i. Br. Das Literaturhaus Basel hat die Idee im Herbst 2014 aufgenommen. Weil das Format bei den Gästen, den GastgeberInnen wie auch den auftretenden AutorInnen so gut ankam, beschloss der Förderfonds Engagement Migros 2015, die Initiative als dreijähriges Pionierprojekt schweizweit zu lancieren. Schweizweit heisst im Moment noch, dass in den Städten Basel und Zürich sowie an verschiedenen Orten des Kantons Aargau und der Zentralschweiz Sofalesungen stattfinden.
