Name: Kimberly Stein Studio
Description:
Creating bold one of a kind artwork. Mixed media on paper along with acrylic paintings focused on the intersection between light, color and shape. 
My pieces are evocative of coastal rhythms and colors.  The saturated colors that nature produces  provides both the influence and the backdrop for my interpretations.  I hope to add a layer of wonder and delight to the gems that the natural world showcases.  
I like to work in large groupings of pieces. So collections will be between 10 and 12 pieces.  They are sold individually or can narrate across the collection.  There will always be a thread that connects the pieces.

Name: Gold Leaf Studios
Description:
Gold Leaf Studios was founded by William Adair in 1982. We are an internationally recognized authority on frame fabrication, conservation, gilding, and large-scale architectural gilding. We provide comprehensive framing services including hand-carved reproduction frames of period styles as well as custom-designed modern frames. Additionally, with our collection of over 3,000 antique frames we offer clients extensive options in choosing an original period frame. We maintain museum standards of conservation, mounting, and matting to protect and enhance artworks. Our clients include private collectors, museums, galleries, historic homes, universities, government agencies, and architectural and interior design firms.

Gold Leaf Studios is located in a 10,000 square foot carriage house in Dupont Circle. Built in 1903 by the Walsh-McLean family, the carriage house is tucked into a courtyard off of Massachusetts Avenue. Our workshop occupies the downstairs space once used as stables. The upper floors include our offices, grand reception room, display areas, and fine art gallery.

Name: 7.5 Club
Description:
Portrait écrit par Grégoire Prangé pour Galeristes 2019 :
"Ce n’est pas une galerie. Proche d’un salon contemporain ou d’un petit centre d’art, mais s’en distinguant toutefois. Tentez de le définir et il vous échappe, tant les formes qui s’y font et défont quotidiennement sortent des habituels cloisonnements du monde de l’art. Fondé en 2011 par Isabelle Suret, le 7.5 club ne cesse de surprendre et d’ouvrir le dia-logue, à la croisée des arts plastiques, de la performance, de la danse, de la musique et de la philosophie. Telle une onde radio l’énergie se diffuse, 7.5 comme hybridation expé-rimentale."

Name: Buck Creek Stained Glass
Description:
Buck Creek Stained Glass is a full-service stained glass studio, led by owner/artist David Schlueter, located in the heart of historic Old Town Helena, just south of Birmingham, Alabama.  

We specialize in custom leaded stained glass windows, leaded stained glass doors, beveled glass doors, and "Tiffany"-style stained glass windows, panels and lamps.  We also offer experienced stained glass restoration and repair.  No piece is too large or small for our talented artists to restore to its former glory.

At Buck Creek, we pride ourselves on our creations - one of a kind works of art that we're happy to sign our names on.  With over 25 years of experience, we work hard to hold ourselves to a high standard of craftsmanship and strive for perfection in even the smallest details - it's the little things that set us apart.

We are located at 4114 Helena Road in Old Town Helena, AL. Our telephone number is (205) 541-3322.  We're open Monday - Friday from 9 AM to 5 PM, and Saturday from 9 AM to 1 PM.  Stop in and see our artists at work or email us at info@buckcreekglass.com.

Name: Ruben Benjamin
Description:
Ruben Benjamin is an artist from Germany. He began working with spray paint and textures at the age of 14, studied mechanical engineering and management, and produces his three-dimensional art in his studio in Munich. Ruben Benjamin’s work deals with perspective, natural phenomena, technology, and motion. 

"To me, in my work, sculpture and painting are interwoven elegantly. I am fascinated by the vibrant luminosity which is achieved by arranging and combining colors in a three-dimensional, overlapping manner. This purism and the provoked contrast of light and shadow affected by the depth of the artworks characterize my work. The frozen dynamics in my textures allow me to capture unique moments in time. Therefore, I describe my work rather as sculptures for walls than paintings.

Name: Pinot's Palette Jeffersonville IN
Description:
Pinot’s Palette – Jeffersonville | Paint. Drink. Have Fun.

Pinot’s Palette – Jeffersonville is Southern Indiana’s newest destination for unforgettable paint-and-sip experiences. Conveniently located in downtown Jeffersonville, our studio blends creativity, entertainment, and community—offering a vibrant space where guests can relax, celebrate, and make memories together.

We host public painting classes, private parties, corporate team-building events, mobile offsite events, kids’ classes, and fundraisers, all led by professional local artists who guide guests step-by-step through each masterpiece. Whether it’s a girls’ night out, date night, family celebration, or corporate retreat, we make creativity accessible and fun for everyone.

Our studio features:

Welcoming, modern art space designed for social creativity

A curated painting library with hundreds of options

Professional instruction for all skill levels—no experience required

Wine, beer, and cocktails available for purchase

Flexible private event options, including mobile events at homes, offices, hotels, or venues

As part of the Pinot’s Palette family, we bring a national brand’s quality together with the heart of the Jeffersonville community. We’re proud to partner with local organizations, nonprofits, and businesses to create experiences that inspire connection and creativity throughout Southern Indiana and the Louisville metro.

Paint. Drink. Have Fun. — now in Jeffersonville.

Name: Rabbit Island Foundation
Description: The Rabbit Island Foundation provides a platform to investigate, expand and challenge creative practices in a remote environment through the Rabbit Island Residency. By living and working on Rabbit Island—a 91 acre wilderness located in the largest freshwater lake in the world, Lake Superior—residents engage directly with the landscape and respond to notions of conservation, ecology, sustainability, and resilience. With the idea that the intelligent organization and celebration of wild spaces is the most civilized thing we can value as society, the residency reflects on the continent’s four hundred year history of settlement and division of land. The island, an unsettled and undivided space, enables residents to present commentary on these ideas, creating creative interpretations and solutions to issues of global importance such as climate change and loss of natural habitat and pristine watersheds.

Name: Relief
Description: In Philebus, Plato remarks that emotions typically include both pleasant and unpleasant aspects. Emotions mentioned in this context are anger, fear, longing, lamentation, love, jealousy, and envy. When the artist creates art, not only is Plato's theory of emotions applied from the emotional standpoint of creation but also the physical aspect. The juxtaposed concepts between texture and stress, composition and escapism, or structure and emotional response is a critical detail to explore when thinking about an artist's identity and the meaning behind their work.

Name: Artesa Latino
Description:
Connecting Cultures Through Art.
Artesa Latino is the premier online gallery dedicated to discovering and championing the most compelling emerging artists from Latin America. Our mission is to build a cultural bridge, connecting the vibrant, untold stories of the region with a discerning global audience of designers and art lovers. We believe in art as a vehicle for connection, identity, and economic empowerment.
Every piece in our collection is meticulously selected, not just for its aesthetic excellence, but for its narrative depth and cultural significance. We operate on a foundational principle of fair trade, ensuring that every acquisition directly supports the artist and their community. This ethical commitment allows our artists to thrive and dedicate themselves to their craft, while providing our collectors with the assurance that their investment makes a tangible impact.
For the conscious art lover, Artesa Latino is a destination for discovery. We offer unique, original works from the next generation of influential artists—a chance to acquire a piece of a story that is destined to appreciate in both cultural and financial value. For interior designers and art advisors, we are a trusted partner in sourcing authentic, statement pieces that bring soul and sophistication to any space.
Join us in celebrating the living roots of Latin American art. Follow our page for artist spotlights, collection previews, and insights into the art market. Explore our curated gallery on our website.

Creativity and Originality

We bring the world to them through story-telling, presentations and discussions that leads on excitement, inspiration, interests and understanding, generating tons of ideas as a community. Artistori's Missions

We inspire individuals to become artists, expressing new ideas, imaginations and feelings through art as a medium, in all art disciplines, in all forms of creativity, style and language, digitally and physically where originality and uniqueness is valued in all masterpieces. Artistori's Vision

• Creativity has no limits.
• Aspiring art teachers and art educators are trained here to drive our mission and follow our vision.

Name: ESSE Academy - Art and Painting courses
Description:
At ESSE Academy, we set ourselves apart from other art courses in the Netherlands by adhering to the time-honored traditions of academic art. Unlike contemporary programs that often dilute technical skills with speculative theories and socio-political discourse, our curriculum is grounded in centuries-old principles that provide a robust and methodical approach to visual arts education.

The range of Art courses we offer are based on professional training and fundamental fine arts disciplines: Drawing, Painting and Composition. Our programme and methods primarily aim to lay the basis for fundamental academic knowledge and visual literacy, nurture a refined sense of artistic expression, cultivate a creative mindset and elevate our followers' aesthetic taste.

Name: ARTIVIAN
Description:
Artivian offers unique team-building art-workshops. lunchtime and after-hours expreriences that promote creativity, stress relief, and deeper connection—all from the comfort of your workplace or our list of chosen venues.

Whether it’s the calming flow of Watercolour, the minimalist focus of Sumi-e ink painting, or the powerful symbolism of Kintsugi—repairing broken pottery with gold—our workshops offer a meaningful, hands-on experience that enhances well-being and team dynamics.

Name: Salvador Arango
Description:
Itaguí, Antioquia, vio nacer, en 1944, al escultor colombiano Salvador Arango. 

El artista adelantó su formación secundaria en el Instituto Nocturno de la Universidad de Antioquia y realizó estudios académicos de pintura, dibujo e historia del arte en el Instituto de Artes Plásticas en la ciudad de Medellín. 
 
Desde 1964, SAAR, seudónimo con el que aparece firmada toda su obra escultórica, partiendo de un neoclasicismo figurativo y dentro de un lento y dispendioso proceso racional y consciente, ha ido penetrando en la simplificación geométrica de sus figuras, hasta lograr un lenguaje claro, personal y contundente. Este concepto geométrico conceptual está constituido por el desplazamiento infinito de sus líneas y la prolongación continua de sus planos en elementos básicos de gran energía, vitalidad y sutileza, para conjugar armoniosamente espacio-forma y movimiento en verdaderas soluciones constructivas, cargadas de luz y humanidad. 
 
Arango, escultor figurativo, investigador y viajero incansable en la búsqueda de nuevas formas de expresión y en la exploración y aplicación de diferentes técnicas y materiales como piedra, mármol, madera, hierro, acero, aluminio y, en especial, fundición en bronce, es considerado en este campo un verdadero experto pues es en esta última técnica en la que ha desarrollado tanto numerosas obras monumentales para sitios públicos en diferentes ciudades de su país como otras obras en menor escala para colecciones privadas en el exterior.

Name: ANTIQUE & CONTEMPORARY ART
Description:
L’histoire du  centre Marval

Les Galeries Marval  sont situées au cœur du centre historique de Neuchâtel et font partie d’un ensemble architectural remontant au XIIème siècle. Elles étaient connues sous le nom de « Cour Marval », du nom de leur premier propriétaire. 
Au cours des siècles, la Cour Marval eut différents propriétaires. En outre, elle a été spectatrice et actrice de l’Histoire de la ville de Neuchâtel tout en restant une construction symbolique pour le Bourg, aimé de tous ses résidents , ainsi que de ses touristes férus d’esthétisme. 
La particularité de ce centre réside aussi dans la fait que de ses pièces anciennes y sont conservées. En effet,  des décorations picturales représentant des scènes de chasse et une colonne qui remonte à l’époque romaine offrent à cet endroit un aspect ancien et donc riche historiquement parlant. 

 La Galerie d’Art
De la peinture à la sculpture, de la photographie à l’art vidéo, des équipements artistiques aux éléments de design (l’ameublement, les vêtements, les bijoux) : la Galerie veut exposer non seulement les œuvres des artistes contemporains, et ce, sous toutes ses formes, mais aussi les œuvres des artistes du passé.
 De cette façon, elle donne la possibilité d’apprécier la dualité  très séduisante de deux mondes qui paraissent de prime abord opposés et qui en définitive,  se laissent admirer selon le cœur et le goût de chacun.
Le lien étroit entre l’Ancien et le Contemporain illustre magnifiquement le mariage entre  la culture italienne exaltante de  son riche passé et le présent innovant et revigorant. 
Le culte de la beauté, la recherche de la forme, la synthèse du design, seront  la base de la philosophie qui inspire les sélections  artistiques des Galeries Marval.

Name: Corporación Cultural Actos Sinestésicos
Description:

Nuestro objetivo es inspirar ideas trascendentes que nos acerquen a un mundo sustentable

Tenemos la convicción de que sentir más, mejorará la forma en la que entendemos nuestro vínculo con la Naturaleza y el Universo. 

A través de un FONDART REGIONAL 2021, fondos del Ministerio de Educación y distintos fondos de la Universidad de Concepción y la Universidad del Bío Bío, hemos manifestado nuestro sueño en Chile, latinoamerica y el mundo.

Name: WALLSPACE GALLERY
Description:
WALLSPACE GALLERY to galeria sztuki współczesnej powstała w 2021 roku.  Mieści się przy ulicy Foksal 15 w Warszawie w odrestaurowanej, secesyjnej kamienicy. Galeria kontynuuje artystyczne tradycje tej ulicy, gdzie znajduje się m.in. Galeria Foksal – niekomercyjna galeria sztuki współczesnej, założona w 1966 roku przez krytyków sztuki i artystów.  

Działalność WALLSPACE GALLERY skupia się na dwóch poziomach. Pierwszym z nich jest promocja polskich klasyków awangardy. Galeria zorganizowała dwie edycje wystawy  pn. “Klasycy Awangardy”, podczas których zaprezentowaliśmy prace takich artystów jak Jan Lebenstein, Tadeusz Brzozowski, Stefan Gierowski, Ryszard Winiarski czy Magdalena Abakanowicz. Ponadto galeria zorganizowała monograficzną wystawę Stanisława Fijałkowskiego z okazji 100. rocznicy urodzin artysty, wystawę prac Henryka Musiałowicza ze zbiorów jego spadkobierców czy wystawę 58 prac Nikifora Krynickiego. Do wspomnianych wystaw wydano drukowany katalog.

Drugim obszarem działalności galerii jest promocja i wspieranie młodych, utalentowanych artystów. WALLSPACE GALLERY reprezentuje takich artystów jak Michał Jackowski, Igor Dobrowolski, Lia Kimura, czy Hubert Dolinkiewicz, którego wystawa dyplomowa zorganizowana była w czerwcu 2023 roku w WALLSPACE. 

WALLSPACE GALLERY organizuje także aukcje dzieł sztuki.

Name: Atelier Pellini
Description:
The Atelier Pellini, a sculpture laboratory since 1903, is now available for events, exhibitions and shootings. 

The idea is to maintain the original identity of the location with the possibility to host cultural events particularly directed to people interested in art, photography, cinema, fashion and performance.

Atelier Pellini is an art location in Milan in via privata Siracusa 6 ( Crocetta/ Porta Romana areas). The sculpture studio was built by Eugenio Pellini at the beginning of the last century. 
In this laboratory Eros Pellini, Eugenio ’s son, learnt from his father the basics of this artistic activity, until he succeeded him in 1934. 
Subsequently, the rooms of the atelier were used not only by Eugenio as a laboratory and a place to exhibit his art but also as a headquarter for Eros wife Rosa Cerri screenplay activities. 
With Eros death in 1993 the Atelier’s artistic activities came to an end. 
Eros daughter Matelda was however able to keep the laboratory intact, giving it a museum-like charm that still today enchants the place. 
Maintaining its true originality and artistic look the atelier is therefore (probably the only) real portrayal of a 1900 artistic laboratory in Milan.

The ideology of the space is grounded on the possibility of coupling the beauty of authenticity and antiquity with the innovation of the present day. Tradition merges with modern artistic experiments. The intent is to use the uniqueness of the location in order to create harmony between artworks from the last century and contemporary ones.

Name: Charlie Hanavich Art
Description:
Charlie attended The University of Alabama, were he studied Advertising and Art History. His passion for art and artistic talent were evident as a small child; however, it wasn’t until his later years in college that Charlie rediscovered his passion for art.

The summer of 2006 was a defining period. Searching for direction in life, Charlie moved to New York for what turned out to be a short-lived visit to his own future. While Charlie didn’t find the business direction he was seeking, he re-discovered his passion for the arts and his future artistic medium of choice; painting.

Hanavich now resides in Miami, Florida where he’s constantly refining his artistic process and techniques. His paintings are often fueled by a combination of personalities, personal experience, and his favorite artists. The influences of Miami's electric art scene continue to provoke new standards in his paintings. 


Charlie’s paintings are displayed in a number of private homes and galleries throughout the United States. For more information about where to find Charlie's work or purchasing an original painting, please email Art@CharlieHanavich.com.

Name: WeartProject
Description:
WEART è crowfunding, art agenda e social Mag, un nuovo modo per supportare, vivere e discutere l'arte

Dagli autori di UnDo.Net in collaborazione con Humanbit ecco WeArt, una piattaforma completamente
nuova che unisce l’informazione sugli eventi culturali al crowdfunding specializzato per l’arte.

Per scoprire l’attualità ma anche la storia degli eventi d’arte e cultura
WeArt offre l’agenda quotidiana degli appuntamenti d’arte e cultura, in Italia e all’estero, costantemente
aggiornata dagli stessi autori e dagli uffici stampa specializzati. WeArt mette a disposizione di appassionati e studiosi un immenso archivio di mostre, eventi, spazi espositivi, artisti e curatori. Uno strumento agilmente navigabile con cui è possibile conoscere in modo approfondito le attività culturali degli ultimi 20 anni. Su WeArt si possono scrivere e condividere opinioni sulle mostre visitate, pubblicare personalmente fotografie e video degli eventi e condividerli sui social.

Ma c’è di più: tramite il crowdfunding è possibile contribuire direttamente alla realizzazione dei progetti!
Per cercare i finanziamenti di un evento culturale e realizzarlo 
WeArt è una piattaforma di crowdfunding per le istituzioni, fondazioni, associazioni e tutte quei soggetti
pubblici e privati no profit che si dedicano alla diffusione, sviluppo e valorizzazione dell’arte. Lo staff di WeArt offre la sua professionalità e la sua passione per aiutare gli organizzatori a definire e trovare i modi più efficaci per sostenere ogni progetto.

Per promuovere il proprio evento
Accreditandosi come Reporter si possono pubblicare personalmente e gratuitamente mostre ed eventi
su WeArt. Si può decidere quando andare online ed arricchire progressivamente i propri comunicati con
video, gallerie di immagini, contenuti dai social.

Name: Herndon Foundation for the Cultural Arts
Description:
The Herndon Foundation for the Cultural Arts (Foundation) was incorporated in 2003 as a nonprofit foundation to promote and support the creation of an Arts Center in downtown Herndon.  Our mission is to establish and sustain the Arts Center through leadership, advocacy, fund raising, programming, policy setting and operation.  Our vision is for the Arts Center to be a gathering place to celebrate the arts in the historic downtown area, a centerpiece of the community with artistic performances, exhibits and educational opportunities where people can celebrate the uplifting and unifying role the arts play in all our lives.

     In an effort to begin arts activities in downtown Herndon, the Foundation raised private funds and renovated the facility rented from the Town of Herndon to create three distinct spaces:  an art gallery with museum quality lighting and a professional art hanging system, an art lab or wet classroom, and a dry classroom or meeting space.  The arts center opened Labor Day Weekend 2008 as ArtSpace Herndon.  Since September 2008, the Foundation has worked with local arts organizations in a collaborative effort to attract quality programming to ArtSpace.  We offer a new visual arts exhibit each month in the Gallery plus classes for adults, teens, and children and arts events.  We have also made the space available to support the activities other local arts organizations and offer rentals to businesses and private citizens for their events.

Name: CIANÓ
Description:

Proporcionamos experiências únicas de criação, estimulando o desenvolvimento artístico por meio da fotografia.

Colaboramos com instituições educacionais, culturais, áreas criativas, estúdios de design e artistas visuais, conectando a cianotipia a diferentes contextos e ampliando seu potencial como ferramenta artística e educativa.

Somos um Laboratório Criativo de Cianotipia que respira arte, ciência, natureza e educação.

Name: Aspect Films
Description: Aspect Films is a creative film studio that produces original content for businesses and brands. We live, and edit, in Vancouver but shoot and produce amazing images from all around the world. Our cinematic exploits have taken us to Poland, South Africa, Hawaii, and Mexico among others. We enjoy pushing the limits of image aquisition technology and employ the Arri Alexa, Red Epic, Vision Research Phantom HD, and 35mm film cameras to achieve the highest quality pictures. We put this technology into the talented hands of some of the world's best cinematographers, and utilize the same post processing facilities as the latest blockbuster films playing at your local cineplex. These efforts ensure results that are worthy of your business or brand's stature and presence, and that your audience will appreciate and revel in. Films are brilliant, brilliant things - and our job is making them.

Name: Government College of Art and Craft, Calcutta
Description: Government College of Art and Craft, Calcutta is one of the oldest and premier institutes of art education in India. Boasting about 159 years legacy, the college has always been a seat of academic excellence. GCAC is a co-educational seat of learning and affiliated to University of Calcutta. Currently the institution follows the Choice Based Credit System (C.B.C.S.) for its undergraduate programmes. From 2005 the institution has also commenced the doctoral degree programme (Ph.D.) in Fine Arts. It is the only fine arts institution in the country to offer a specialized course in Painting indian style. The institution has received an ‘A’ from the NAAC accreditation committee in 2007.
